The ink still smells like rain on pavement,
but the stamps curl,
unlicked.
I trace your name with my thumb,
wondering if the address still exists.
Some words are too heavy to mail,
too fragile to trust the mailbox.
They sit,
breathing,
in the dark.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
You’re staring at the ceiling again.
The cracks look like roots
searching for something to hold.
You count them,
but they keep growing.
Some nights,
you swear they whisper your name.
Not the one you answer to,
the one you buried under routine.
The alarm rings,
and you press snooze
on the part of you that wanted to climb trees.

Grief isn’t crying anymore.
It’s the way your coffee goes cold,
unnoticed.
The kitchen light hums,
but you don’t hear it.
SLOWLY,
the weight of their absence
becomes a second shadow,
walking beside you.
You don’t turn to look.
It doesn’t ask for answers.
Just teaches you how to carry what’s left.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being the One Who Always Checks In
scripts.script:
There's a kind of loyalty that hums.
Like a fridge in another room,
steady,
unseen.
I check the forecast for your mood
before I check my own.
The messages pile
soft as laundry you'll never fold,
but I smooth them anyway.
Some people need a lighthouse.
You just need the fog to lift sometimes.
I'm the one holding the flashlight,
pointing at nothing.
send this to them.

The tide doesn’t apologize,
for pulling what it gave,
just whispers,
come back tomorrow,
and the shells,
will be different,
but just as temporary.
Some days,
you’ll find one,
perfectly cracked,
and know,
it was never meant to last,
and that’s the gift.

You’re not lost.
Just untethered,
like a balloon
that forgot to be afraid of the sky.
Some mornings,
you wake up lighter,
and the weight isn’t gone—
it’s just yours now.
The hands that once held you
let go,
but the air still holds you up.
You’re not floating.
You’re learning how to fly.
Let them watch.

I stopped counting the nights.
Your name fits in my palm like a pebble,
smooth from years of holding.
The bed never feels empty,
just softer where you aren’t.
I trace the shape of your absence,
and the sheets remember your weight.
Some bodies leave echoes,
others leave holes.
You left a rhythm.
I hum it when the lights go out.
send this to them.

Some friendships are anchors,
not ropes,
keeping you steady
when the world tilts.
You were the weight
that never pulled me under,
just held me close
to the ocean floor,
where the light still reaches.
I didn’t know how deep we were
until the tide changed,
and I could still breathe.
More than my favorite person—
you were the gravity.
I miss the pull.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
You’re stirring honey into tea.
The spoon spins,
but the liquid doesn’t swirl—
it just parts for you,
like the ocean for a saint.
I watch the way your wrist tilts,
how the amber pools without sound.
No one else notices,
but the air hums differently here.
The cup warms my palms,
and I realize—
this is how you turn ordinary into sacred.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
The porch light stayed on all summer.
I never turned it off,
just watched the moths circle,
soft wings brushing the glass like unanswered questions.
They never learned,
kept coming back,
night after night,
to the same warm glow.
I wonder if they remember the dark.
The bulb burned out yesterday.
I left it there,
send this to them.

The tide doesn’t apologize,
for pulling back,
leaving shells breathless on the sand.
Some people,
like the moon,
call you closer,
but never let you orbit.
You learned,
to shine,
without drowning in their gravity.
Now,
you glow,
in your own eclipse.
visual_keywords: single conch shell on wet sand

scripts.topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
scripts.script:
An old gardener whispered—
roots stretch
beyond the pot you planted them in.

they don’t ask permission
to seek deeper soil.

maybe your friendship was a season
a shared path that split at the ridge
one climbs toward sun the other toward rain
and neither is wrong.

you’ll meet again in the valley
as strangers who once knew each other’s names.

You're folding their sweater again.
The wool still smells like rain,
but the scent is fading.
Not in a rush,
just the way old book pages yellow,
slowly turning to dust.
You don’t hate the color,
just the way it doesn’t fit anymore.
Some memories,
like mismatched socks,
are easier to lose.
You’ll donate it tomorrow.
Or maybe next week.

The pillow still holds your shape
slowly 
   Forgotten memories resurface 
   IN THE DEPTHS of my mind 
   a whispered promise, 
   and the weight of it 
   SINKS LIKE ANCHORS 
   but gently 
   THE RAIN FALLS 
   on empty pages 
   and the words we never said 
   remain unspoken 
   in this quiet space.

How do you explain the way
light lingers on your mother’s hands
when she thinks no one’s watching,
but the flour still clings to her skin
like a secret she’s too tired to hide?
The kitchen hums,
low and steady,
not asking for anything,
just the weight of small things—
a cracked mug,
a half-peeled potato,
the way time bends
around what matters most.
Send this to her.

scripts.topic: Being Tired in Ways Sleep Cannot Fix
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
but the steam won’t rise.
I stir the sugar,
slowly,
like I’m dissolving,
not sweetening.
The cup stays cold,
just like the weight in my ribs,
not heavy,
just... there.
Some mornings,
you wake up hollow,
and no amount of light
fills the space.
let them sleep.

There's a quiet like the first snowfall,
not cold,
just waiting to be named.
I built a house inside my ribs,
four walls of breath,
and locked the door.
Some rooms I never showed anyone,
not even the mirror.
I learned the shape of my own hunger,
how it hums,
not like a storm,
but like a river finding its bed.
I belong to the current now.
Let them watch me carry the light.
visual_keywords: single candle in a vast room

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
Some feelings don’t ask for permission.
They arrive like the tide,
unnoticed until your shoes are wet.
You try to step back,
but the water remembers your name.
Not in waves,
just a slow pull,
dragging you deeper.
The sand beneath you shifts,
and you realize
it’s not the ocean you’re afraid of—
it’s the weight of wanting.
Let them stay dry.

There's a quiet like a locked door—
no one knocks,
just tests the handle,
assumes it's stuck.
I leave the key
where they can't see it,
but they never look.
The walls hum
with things I never said,
and the echo answers for me.
I am a language
you chose not to learn.
send this to them.

You’re not empty. Just waiting.
The air hums,
low and steady,
like a cat purring against your ribs.
No one sees the weight you carry—
not the bricks,
but the way you balance them.
Stillness isn’t surrender.
It’s the moment before the tree decides to bend.
Your breath,
a quiet rebellion.
The world moves,
you choose to stay.
Send this to them.

YOU LEAVE THE LIGHT ON
in the hallway
just in case
I wake up scared
and for years after
I still check
before I sleep.

SOMEONE TAUGHT YOU
how to fold a fitted sheet
so it lies flat
like a promise
and now every corner
feels like home.

THE WAY YOU
scrunch your nose
when you laugh
is the first thing
I ever memorized.

I FIND YOUR HAIR
in my sweater pockets
months later
and it still smells
like the kitchen
where you made toast
at 2 a.m.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
You don’t glow. Not like that.
The way your socks never match,
but your toes curl when you laugh—
I’ve memorized the pattern.

Your coffee cup leaves rings,
perfect circles on my notebook,
like tiny secrets you didn’t mean to tell.

The way you hum off-key,
just slightly,
and the song still fits.

Your shadow on the wall,
taller than you are,
but softer.
Send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered—
not everything soft
bends to your will.

some things grow
only where the soil
is left undisturbed.

you can dig and dig,
but the seed knows
when the sun is right.

what you force
never takes root—
what waits,
does.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
You’re folding their favorite sweater
again
just to feel the weight of it
The sleeves still smell like yesterday
but the clock ticks louder now
Some things don’t need words
to know they’re ending
You press the fabric to your face
slowly
like a secret you can’t keep
The door is closing
and you’re still standing there
holding onto threads.

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
Lao Tzu whispered
what bends you
is not the wind
but the shape
of your branches.

you cannot still the storm
but you can learn
to dance in the rain
without naming it
or blaming the sky.

let the weight settle
where it lands.

We are rivers,
carving canyons through our own ribs.
Every ache,
a chisel,
shaping us deeper,
not to break,
but to hold more light.
The weight you carry,
becomes the bridge
others cross in the dark.
Some wounds,
are just doors,
left ajar.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
There's a kind of strength that hums
like a fridge in an empty kitchen.
You keep the lights on,
but the bulb flickers only for you.
They praise your steady hands,
never noticing the cracks you patch with spit and silence.
The house stands,
the walls breathe,
and you,
you are the only one who hears the foundation sigh.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You’re not hiding.
Just resting your face,
and the world gets lighter.
The way your fingers
fidget with the edge of the curtain,
not nervous,
just remembering how sunlight feels.
I’ve seen you like this before—
unbraced,
unarmored,
like a door left ajar.
Some secrets only exist
when no one’s looking.
I’ll keep this one.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
It’s not devotion. Not like that.
Your name tastes like rain on old paper,
soft edges,
fading ink I trace with my thumb.
I DON’T SAY IT TO HOLD YOU,
just to watch the way your eyelashes
catch the light when you’re not looking.
And... it’s not about forever,
just the quiet hum of your sweater
when you lean against me,
like a secret I’m not supposed to keep.
send this to them.

The face I wear is paper,
thin,
fragile as a moth’s wing in winter.
Every laugh I stitch,
every grin I fold,
just another crease in the mask.
Some days,
it clings like wet silk,
others,
it peels back,
revealing the ink beneath.
Not sadness,
not fear,
just the quiet hum of being seen,
finally,
without explanation.

Real love isn’t a promise,
it’s a practice.
Every morning,
you wake up,
choose again.
The hands that built the bridge
can also burn it,
but they don’t.
Some people carry storms inside,
not to destroy,
but to water the seeds
you forgot you planted.
visual_keywords: a single lit lantern in fog

scripts.topic: The Kind of Love That Doesn't Shout
scripts.script:
IT LIVES in the way
she stirs the pot
one slow circle
then tastes
without asking if it's good.
The salt finds its way
like we do,
through back doors
and half-open windows.
WE NEVER NAME IT
but the spoons know,
the ones we leave
in the sink overnight.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
You hand me a mug
warm
and the steam
curls like a question
I never learned to answer.
WE STOOD IN THE KITCHEN
counting cracks in the ceiling
while the coffee went cold.
It wasn’t special
until you looked at me
like I was the first light
you’d seen in years.
Now every morning tastes
of something lost
and found
all at once.

YOU WERE THE ONE
who knew
where the light switches hid,
who left the porch bulb burning
when I came home late.
I never asked,
you just...
remembered.
The way you traced the cracks in the sidewalk
like constellations,
and suddenly
every broken thing
was a story worth keeping.

Saturday afternoon. The screen glows blue.
Your name appears,
then disappears,
like a tide pulling back.
I still set the table for two,
but the chair stays empty.
Some friendships are mirrors,
reflecting only what you need to see.
The messages you ignore
become echoes in my inbox,
slowly dissolving into static.
I won’t delete your number,
just the expectation.
Let it go.

I stopped counting the rings.
Each one holds a secret,
whispered to the bark.
The oldest trees remember
what we keep forgetting:
Happiness isn’t a place.
It’s the slow curve of moss
climbing your skin,
the way roots know
where the light will be,
long before the leaves.
I press my palm here,
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
scripts.script:
You don’t carry the world. It carries you.
The bones in your back curve like a question,
asking the sky for one honest answer.
They never taught you how to put it down,
so you learned to stack it neatly,
like folded laundry no one claims.
The weight doesn’t bruise—
it just presses,
leaving the shape of you slightly different.
Some days you wake up lighter,
but the air remembers.
Send this to them.

YOU KNOW THE WAY
someone’s laugh
lingers
like the last train’s hum
after it’s gone.
The way they leave
crumbs of warmth
on every surface
YOU TOUCH
and suddenly
every room
feels like coming home.

Words linger
we just
unsent messages
piling up somewhere
in the depths of our phones
AND YET WE HOLD ON
still
to the hope
that someday
SOMEONE WILL UNDERSTAND
the weight of what we didn't say

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
Tuesday at 4:17.
The kettle hums,
but the room stays cold.
I trace your name
in steam on the window,
not for you to see,
just to feel the shape of it.
Some words
are too heavy to speak,
so I leave them
on the counter like loose change,
hoping you’ll pick them up,
but you never do.

Borrowed love never fits right.
The seams show,
even when you stitch it tight.
Some hands,
just hold what isn’t theirs to keep—
cupping water,
watching it slip through.
You’ll smile,
but the weight shifts,
and the borrowed light dims.
Not stolen,
just returned.
Let it go.

scripts.topic: The Last Text You Never Sent
scripts.script:
You delete the draft again.
The screen glows like a moonlit puddle,
reflecting nothing but your hesitation.
Tiny ripples,
where your fingers touched the glass.
The words are still there,
hovering just beneath the surface.
You could reach in,
pluck them out like drowned stars.
But the water’s too cold,
and some constellations belong to the dark.
Send this to them.

There's a pride that hums
softly beneath the skin,
like a second pulse you've earned.
You stop apologizing for sunlight,
for taking up space,
for the way your laughter spills over.
The mirror no longer flinches,
and that,
is the quietest revolution.
Your shadow stretches taller,
unafraid of its own shape.
This isn't defiance.
This is simply you,
learning to stay.

You peel the tape off your skin.
Tiny squares of paper cling,
like memories you can’t shake.
The scissors lie still on the desk,
blades cold.
You cut the strings,
but the kite still tugs at your ribs.
Breathe.
The room doesn’t ask for explanations.
Your shadow stretches,
long and patient,
waiting for you to sit with it.
Send this to them.

The lamp stays on all night.
It knows your shape better than you do,
soft edges where the light spills,
and the way your shadow stretches
when you finally exhale.
Breathe in the quiet gold,
that warm halo you mistake for safety.
But it’s just you,
learning the weight of your own name,
letting it sit on your tongue
like the first sip of something sweet.
Send this to yourself.

I KNEW IT WAS WRONG
to keep you
to keep you close
without ever saying
without ever touching
without ever even looking
YOU WERE THE SECRET
I carried
in the back of my throat
like a swallowed coin
still warm
from someone else’s pocket
and every time I spoke
I saved the last word
for you

A Japanese gardener whispered
not the branch that bends
but the one that snaps
under snow
learns nothing.

I watched an old pine
sway without apology
roots deep in shifting earth
— it never asks the wind to stop.

You can hold a bird
but not its flight
let the sky teach you
how to be empty
how to be full.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
We built the ladder
one rung
at a time
not knowing
if the sky would hold our weight.
The stars blinked anyway,
indifferent
but beautiful.
WE KEPT CLIMBING.
Somewhere between the cracks
we found constellations
no one else could name.

YOU KNEW EXACTLY
where to press
to make me bend.
I was the chair
that held your weight
while you tied your shoes
and walked away.
Still warm,
still shaped like you,
I wait
for the next pair of hands
to need me.

I stopped counting the steps to your door.
The way the floorboards sigh underfoot,
like an old cat settling in sunlight.
I never told you
this is the only place
where my shoulders remember how to drop.
The kettle hums a lullaby,
and suddenly,
I’m eight years old again,
safe inside a story I haven’t finished.
Even the air tastes different here—
like rain on warm stone.
send this to them.

How do you explain to someone
that your shadows aren’t sadness—
they’re just echoes of light
that never reached them?
I’ve tried to stretch my arms
but the room keeps shrinking,
and no one notices
the walls are made of their assumptions.
Some voices wrap around me like fog,
slowly erasing the edges of what I meant.
I won’t fight to be seen.
Let them call it mystery.
I know it’s just the shape of me
when no one’s looking.

There's a quiet like unopened mail.
The kind that sits,
waiting for fingers to break the seal.
You were the letter I didn’t know I needed,
folded inside my own address.
Every word you wrote
shifted the ink of my days.
I trace your sentences
when the room feels too still.
Some changes don’t announce themselves,
they simply rewrite the margins.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
What I mean is the tide,
low and listening,
carrying shells like secrets,
not to keep,
just to turn them,
see how the light bends inside.
Some nights,
the moon is a coin,
slipped between my ribs,
not payment,
just weight,
just knowing,
I’m held by something vast.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
The porch light stays on all night.
A habit,
not a warning.
Some souls collect moments like loose change,
counting them at midnight
while others spend theirs on sunlight,
buying time with laughter.
The clock doesn’t care if you’re breathing,
only if you’re awake enough to hear it.
but the wind remembers your name,
whispers it through the screen door,
and for a second,
you’re more than just air passing through.

scripts.topic: When You Have to Pretend You Are Fine at Work
scripts.script:
You’re not tired. Just hollow.
The coffee cup stays full,
but the steam never rises.
Some mornings,
you button the same shirt twice,
forgetting which one’s real.
The clock hums,
a quiet machine counting nothing.
You laugh,
and the sound lands wrong,
like a coin dropped in sand.
No one notices.
They never see the suitcase
still packed under your desk.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
Some mornings taste like old book pages.
The coffee steams,
but it’s the way you stirred,
slow and absent,
that lingers like a fingerprint on the mug.
I still reach for the sugar bowl twice,
just to feel the ghost of your habit.
The clock ticks,
and suddenly,
every second is a memory I didn’t ask for.
Some people don’t leave,
they just stop being the weather.
I miss the forecast.
visual_keywords: a clock with a second hand frozen mid-swing

scripts.script:
The river doesn’t apologize,
for carving its own path.
It bends,
but never breaks,
just learns to flow around the rocks.
Some roots grow deeper,
when the soil forgets to ask permission.
I am not the shore,
I am the current,
carrying what I choose,
leaving what I outgrow,
without drowning in the weight.

4:17 p.m. The clock hums.
You’re the pause
between my inhales,
steady as the tide
that never asks permission.
I don’t float,
yet I’m weightless,
carried by something
quieter than trust.
Some days I forget
you’re not the air—
you’re the space it fills.
Stay.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone You Cannot Have
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that eats the light,
not from absence,
but from knowing,
The plate is full,
and still,
your hands hover over empty air.
Some doors don't creak,
they simply dissolve,
leaving only the shape of what passed through,
I won't starve,
just learn to live,
in the echo of a feast.

Some nights I trace the cracks,
not on the ceiling,
just the ones in my voice.
I whisper your name,
but it lands like a leaf
on still water,
no splash,
no ripple,
just a quiet sinking.
The room doesn’t notice,
and neither do you,
but the dark holds it anyway.

There's a kind of stillness that breathes,
like the space between piano keys.
Your arms,
a harbor I didn’t chart.
No lighthouse needed,
just the quiet hum of your ribs
against my ear.
I memorized the shape of safety
in the way you hold a coffee cup,
steady,
unshaken.
The world could tilt,
but here—
your collarbone is my gravity.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Part You Never Talk About
scripts.script:
The way your hands still shake
when you reach for the light.
WE ALL HAVE ONE THING
that hums in the dark.
You never name it
never let it out
but it curls around your ribs
like a second shadow.
SOME NIGHTS
you swear it breathes
right against your ear
and you don’t flinch.
It’s the part that knows
you’re already forgiven.

YOU WERE WEARING
that sweater
I’d later steal
when it rained
and the bus stop smelled like wet wool,
old gum,
your laugh
stuck to my ribs
like a second shadow
I didn’t know
was mine
until you left.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
still warm from the last cup you made.
I trace the steam,
slowly rising,
like the words I swallowed whole.
The kitchen light flickers,
not from the bulb,
but from the weight of what I didn’t say.
Some nights,
the silence isn’t empty—
it’s full of echoes,
and I’m the only one who hears them.
I won’t rewrite the ending,
just the part where I stayed quiet.
Send this to them.

You trace the cracks in your coffee mug.
The ones I never noticed.
They spiderweb like secrets
only porcelain keeps.
I wear my grin like a borrowed scarf,
soft against your skin,
but the tag still scratches my neck.
You count the freckles on my shoulder,
connect them into constellations
I didn’t know I carried.
The stars in your eyes are quiet,
but they’re naming me anyway.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Kind of Love That Doesn't Shout
scripts.script:
A vine once whispered
to the stone beneath it
not I need you
but I choose you.

no roots cling
no branches beg
only the slow
unseen embrace
that holds without holding.

you’ll know it
when the light lingers
a second longer
than it has to
and no one asks why.

I still hold my breath
when your name appears
like a kite tangled in power lines
no one else sees it
but I watch the way it trembles
not begging for rescue
just existing
Some days I forget to check
and the wind carries you further
I won’t pull the string
let the sky keep you

I stopped waiting by the phone
at 3:17,
the exact time you last called.
Not because I gave up,
but because the dust settled
on the receiver,
and I realized
no one was coming to wipe it clean.
Some apologies don’t arrive,
they evaporate mid-air,
leaving only the weight of what could’ve been.
I still press redial sometimes,
just to hear the dial tone,
steady and unchanging,
like my regret.

Real love doesn’t ask for much
it only
needs your hands
steady when the world tilts
YOUR VOICE
soft in the dark
when the ceiling cracks
not to fix
just to say
i’m here
and mean it
until the light comes back.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You are not hiding. Just resting.
The way your eyelashes brush your cheek,
like a moth testing the dark.
I count the freckles you never notice,
constellations only I have named.
Your lips part,
slightly,
and I wonder what small thought
is living there,
unspoken.
The room holds its breath.
send this to them.

Some nights,
the moon stitches your name
into the dark,
thread by thread,
and I pretend not to notice.
The air hums,
a low current
only I can feel,
carrying your voice
like a secret I was never meant to keep.
I trace the shape of you
in the condensation on my window,
knowing the sun will erase it
before morning,
but tracing anyway.

4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
but the water never boils.
Some lessons only arrive
when the house forgets to breathe.
The clock’s hands,
slowly learning to bend,
show me how to carry time,
not count it.
Grief isn’t a shadow,
it’s the light left in the room
after the bulb flickers out.
I won’t ask it to leave,
just teach me to sit
in the glow.

YOU TAUGHT ME
that some doors
only open
inward.
I pushed
until my palms bruised,
until the hinges screamed,
until I realized
the lock was never broken—
just not meant
for me.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
What I mean is the way,
the tide forgets,
to ask permission before it rises.
Some nights,
I am the moon,
not pulling,
just existing,
and the water still answers.
I mean the quiet conspiracy,
of roots,
that hold the whole tree up,
without ever being seen.
I mean,
I am learning,
to be the soil,
not the storm.

There's a kind of ache that wears a smile.
The clock ticks,
but the hands don’t move.
Your coffee goes cold
while you rehearse the script—
"I’m fine, just tired."
The mirror reflects
someone who looks like you,
but the eyes are rehearsed.
You laugh at nothing,
and the sound is perfect.
No one notices the seams.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
You’re holding the railing too tight.
Your knuckles,
pale as the streetlights above,
and no one asks
if the weight is too much.
Some days,
you’re the bridge
that everyone crosses,
but the cracks
are swallowing your name.
You laugh,
and the sound is a coin
dropped into a well—
no echo,
just silence pretending to listen.
Let them walk.

How do you name the moment
when the mirror stops asking questions?
You just stand there,
not smiling,
not frowning,
just letting the light settle
like dust on old books.
Some mornings,
the quiet isn’t empty—
it’s a room you’ve finally learned to live in.
The walls don’t judge,
the floor holds your weight,
and the door stays open.
You don’t have to leave.
This is where you’ve been all along.

I FORGOT HOW TO ASK
for help
so I built a ladder
from the splinters
of every door I closed.
The steps creaked
like old bones
learning to dance again.
By the top
the wind
whispered my name
and I answered
without flinching.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
Grief isn’t the weight you drop.
It’s the pocket you sew
into every coat,
just enough space
to hold what’s gone
without tipping the scales.
Some mornings,
you forget it’s there—
until the wind shifts,
and the fabric brushes your wrist,
reminding you
it’s still light enough to carry.

There's a hunger that eats,
not for food,
but for the shape of their voice.
You memorize the cracks,
the way it breaks on certain words,
and call it music.
Some people don't feed you,
they just teach you how to starve quietly.
The hands that never held yours
still leave fingerprints on your ribs,
and you trace them,
like constellations of what could've been.
You don't miss them,
just the version of you
that believed in their light.
visual_keywords: shadows stretching across a bare wall
a single lit window in a dark neighborhood

scripts.script:
I still set the table for two
quiet rituals no one sees
The chair across from me stays warm
just from memory’s weight
Some nights I pour two glasses of rain
and drink both in the dark
not to forget
you’re still the hand I reach for in thunder
The clock doesn’t move
but the dust on your coat does
I won’t brush it off
let it settle like you did
send this to them.

FREEDOM unfurls
softly
in the creases
of our worn-out expectations
SLOWLY we learn
to let go
of the need
to control every outcome
AND INSTEAD
we find peace
in the gentle hush
of acceptance

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
How do you name the space between,
the way your fingers
leave dents in the couch cushion,
not from weight
but from waiting.
I notice the way you stir your coffee,
three slow circles,
pause,
then lift the spoon like it’s fragile.
Some days,
I swear I can hear the air shift
when you exhale,
like the room itself
is holding its breath.
Tell them.

Hafiz knew this—
the ones who take
leave fingerprints on your kindness.
not scars,
just smudges of their own hurry.

you learn to wipe them clean
not with anger,
but the same cloth you use
for dew on morning grass.

some hands are cups,
some are sieves.
know the difference,
and pour anyway.
visual_keywords: empty teacup on a wooden table

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
YOU TILT YOUR HEAD
just
like the old porch swing
when the rope starts to fray
I forget how to blink
HOW THE LIGHT
slips through your lashes
like a secret I’m not supposed to keep
but I do
I always do
And for a second
everything else
fades to static

THE WAY YOU STAND
still
like the last train
holding its breath
before the station empties.

You don’t flinch
when the wind tests you
or when the world
shifts its weight.

LEAVES fall
around your feet
but never touch you—
you were the ground
all along.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
A Zen master whispered
to the crowded room—
breathing is not waiting.
You inhale the world
but do you taste the rain?

I stood by the river
after dawn,
watched a heron stab the shallows—
no hesitation,
just the slow uncoiling of hunger.

We mistake endurance for life,
count the hours like coins in a jar,
forget the weight of a single ripe peach
in the palm.

YOU REMEMBER THE WAY
I’d set the table
for two
before you even thought of coming

I’d leave the porch light on
past midnight
past reason

YOU NEVER ASKED FOR IT
but I gave
anyway
like a tide
that keeps returning
to a shore
that doesn’t want the water

now the plates gather dust
and the bulb burns out
visual_keywords: empty chair at a set table

scripts.script:
The last text still glows on your screen.
Unanswered,
but not unread.
I trace the letters like a child
learning a new language.
The backspace button hums,
soft as a lullaby.
One tap,
and the weight lifts.
Not erased,
just... set down.
The phone dims,
quietly,
like a door closing behind me.

I leave the porch light on
for you
just in case
you ever drive by
slow enough to see
it flicker like a Morse code
I never learned to send.
The neighbors think I’m forgetful
but really
I’m waiting
for the hum of tires
on wet pavement
to sound like your name.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
The suitcase stays half-packed for weeks.
Not out of hesitation,
just the quiet weight of what fits inside.
Some memories
refuse to fold neatly,
stubborn as a sleeve caught in the zipper.
I trace the seams,
counting the miles between stitches,
but the distance isn’t in the fabric.
It’s in the way the light shifts
when you walk out the door.
I’ll leave the porch light on.
Not to guide you back,
just so you know
someone still sees the dark.

scripts.topic: The Exhaustion of Pretending to Be Okay
scripts.script:
You’re not tired. Just hollow.
The same smile you rehearse in the mirror,
slowly cracks under fluorescent light.
No one asks,
and you don’t tell—
just nod,
like a dashboard bobblehead in rush hour.
Some days,
even gravity feels like an audience.
The weight of your own bones,
a standing ovation you didn’t ask for.
You’re not okay.
You’re just better at lying to yourself now.

When you found me,
I was a half-built bridge
suspended over nothing,
just steel whispers
and the wind’s warning.
You didn’t ask for directions,
just brought a toolbox
and a silence that knew how to listen.
Some nights,
the bolts still hum your name,
but the river underneath?
It never once asked for a map.

You are not the rain. Not even close.
A single drop on the windowpane,
and suddenly I’m breathing again.
The room doesn’t change,
but the air tastes lighter.
Your voice doesn’t fix anything,
it just makes the cracks feel intentional.
Like kintsugi,
but the gold is your name.
I don’t need you to hold me,
just let me lean.
The quiet hum of your presence,
that’s the only lullaby I’ve ever known.
send this to them.

The porch light stays on all night.
You taught it to wait,
but it never learned how to turn off.
YOUR SMILE IS A LOCKED JOURNAL,
pages filled with ink that won’t bleed.
I trace the spine,
wondering if the key is lost
or just hidden in plain sight.
The corners are soft now,
worn from hands that pretend not to notice.
I leave it on the shelf,
where the dust settles like a secret.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
YOU LEAVE the porch light on
for me
warm yellow spilling down the steps
like an invitation
I step over the threshold
and the door clicks shut
not locked
just held
YOU STIR the pot one more time
add a pinch of salt
taste it
say nothing
I count the freckles on your wrist
while you pretend not to notice
the way my fingers linger
WE DON’T talk about forever
we just
keep choosing

The river knows,
it doesn’t fight the bend,
just carves a softer path.
Some stones,
they don’t resist the current,
they become the quiet shape of patience.
The hands that push,
only muddy the water,
while the ones that wait,
watch the silt settle into clarity.
Not giving up,
just letting the world,
show you where to go.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
How do you name the moment
when the seatbelt light turns off,
and no one reaches for your hand?
You were never the destination,
just the detour they took
when the highway felt too long.
Some people don’t keep you,
they keep the idea of you,
a postcard they tuck in their pocket
until the ink runs dry.
I won’t ask for a window seat,
I’ll fold my hands in my lap,
and watch the clouds pass.
You were never the flight,
just the turbulence they braced for.

They hear the words,
but not the weight.
Like stones dropped in a well,
splash,
then swallowed whole.
Some people,
build cages from your echoes.
The hands that,
reach for you,
only touch the shadow.
I am a language,
no one bothers to translate.
Yet,
even the tide,
misunderstands the moon.

scripts.topic: Why Peace Is Not the Absence of Storm
scripts.script:
PEACE IS NOT THE SKY
when it clears,
but the way
we keep walking
through the downpour
with our shoes untied,
knowing the puddles
will rise
and still
we step in,
letting the water
hold us
for a second.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
Broken things don’t always need repair.
Some cracks let the light in,
not as a flaw,
but as proof.
You didn’t polish my edges,
just held the pieces
while I learned to glow.
The weight of staying
isn’t in fixing,
but in refusing to look away.
I didn’t heal,
I just stopped hiding.
Thank you for seeing the shape of me.

You don’t need saving.
Not like this.
The way you hold the door open,
quiet,
just long enough for me to slip through—
that’s the kind of anchor
I’ve been searching for.
Some people leave footprints,
you leave whole paths,
worn smooth by trust.
I won’t ask you to stay,
but I’ll always choose the road
that leads back to you.

Hafiz whispered
some souls
are like rain
that never quite reaches the ground,
hovering just above your skin
a promise you can almost taste.
I keep wondering
if the one who left
was ever meant to stay
or just to teach me
how to hold what evaporates
without regret.

scripts.topic: Grieving Someone Who Did Not Die
scripts.script:
The house still breathes your cologne,
but the keys don’t turn,
the locks have learned your shape.
Some nights,
the faucet drips your name,
steady,
like a metronome counting absence.
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
map them to the last words you didn’t say,
just shadows,
stretching longer than the silence.
The bed is a tide,
pulling me under,
but the shore is gone.

The suitcase still smells like salt air.
Not from a trip,
just the way you packed it—
slowly,
like you knew it wouldn’t be coming back.
Some doors don’t creak when they close,
they just sigh,
and the hallway gets a little darker.
But the keys are still warm in your palm,
and the lock turns easier than you thought.
Not a new start,
just the courage to walk through.
Let them know.

The porch light flickered once at 3:17 a.m.
You didn’t ask why I knew the exact time.
A single moth circled the bulb,
its wings brushing the glass like a secret.
I counted the seconds between beats,
and suddenly,
you were the only rhythm that made sense.
Your laugh,
a quiet hum in the dark,
became the dial tone I wanted to answer forever.
The air smelled like cut grass and rain,
but all I could taste was the sugar on your lips.
We didn’t say a word.
The light burned out before morning.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
scripts.script:
There's a devotion that doesn't cling,
not chains,
just roots,
deep in the soil of your absence.
The leaves still turn toward you,
but the wind doesn't ask why.
Some hands build shelters,
and some hands,
quietly,
learn to hold the rain.
I won't call it sacrifice,
just the shape of my palms,
when you're not here.
Send this to them.

I kept your voice on my phone
too long
too quiet
like a secret I couldn’t delete.
YOU NEVER KNEW
I played it back
on rainy afternoons
when the house was empty
and the walls leaned in.
Now the file’s gone
but the echo still hums
in the spaces between my ribs.
visual_keywords: fingers hovering over a phone screen
a hand pressing against a chest

scripts.script:
You’re folding the laundry again.
not because it’s dirty
but because it’s something to do
with your hands.
The sleeves still smell like their cologne,
but slowly
you stop pressing them to your face.
Some nights,
you leave the basket half-empty
and walk outside.
The air isn’t lighter,
just different.
You’re learning to breathe it anyway.
not for them.
not for anyone.
just the quiet click
when the door locks behind you.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like static after rain,
softly humming in the walls.
I never told you
I memorized the way your sleeves
folded at the wrists,
like secrets tucked just out of reach.
The space between us
wasn’t empty—
it was full of things I swallowed whole.
Your voice on the phone
sounded like pages turning,
and I never said
your name tasted like morning tea.
send this to them.

4:17 a.m. The kettle hums one last note.
It’s not the water that’s boiling,
just the questions you stopped asking.
Every cup tastes the same now,
bitter,
like the name you no longer recognize.
The mirror holds a stranger’s gaze,
and you nod,
not out of agreement,
but exhaustion.
Some days,
you forget to wear your own skin.
Let them wonder.
visual_keywords: steam curling from a chipped mug
fingers tracing condensation on a window

scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The fridge hums low.
I trace the cracks,
not the promises.
Some words,
just echoes in a hallway,
never finding the right door.
Your voice,
still tangled in the sheets,
but the bed’s been made.
I won’t ask for rain,
just the forecast.
The sky stayed clear.
send this to them.

You’re holding a dandelion clock,
steady,
not yet ready to let go.
The seeds are whispers,
light enough to carry,
but heavy with what’s next.
Some moments,
you cling to,
like childhood pockets full of stones,
until your hands ache.
Then,
the wind takes one,
and you realize,
losing is just another way of giving.
Let it fly.

The person who stayed
isn’t a shadow,
just the shape of the door left open.
Every time you walk through,
you don’t notice
how the hinges remember your weight,
how the air shifts,
just slightly,
to make room.
Some nights,
the house breathes slower,
waiting for the click of the latch,
but never locking.

scripts.topic: The Text You Read But Did Not Reply To
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the hours
since your name lit up my screen.
The message still glows,
a soft blue pulse in the dark,
like a jellyfish drifting
through currents I can’t swim.
I trace the words with my thumb,
but the tide keeps pulling me under.
Some secrets are too fragile to answer.
They float there,
waiting for the moon to rise.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Healing Hurts More Than the Wound
scripts.script:
The body remembers,
even when the mind forgets.
Stitches pull,
not to close,
but to teach the skin its shape again.
Some scars,
bright as new constellations,
ache with the weight of stars.
The night you stopped bleeding,
you learned how heavy light can be.
And the hands that held you down,
now tremble,
not from fear,
but from the effort of holding on.

the heron knows
nothing is lost
only rearranged
into the next tide
into the next sky.

you didn’t change the world
you changed the way
the light falls on the water
and suddenly
every reflection
is a new beginning

the ripples remember you.

I stopped believing in luck
when your shadow stretched over mine
Not a rescue
just a quiet collision of two lost orbits
The way you held my wrist
had the weight of an old promise
but your fingers trembled like leaves before rain
Somewhere between your hesitation and my surrender
we became something temporary and permanent
I won’t name what we were
just know I still trace the constellations of your freckles
when the night feels too wide
send this to them.

THE WAY YOU HOLD YOURSELF
when the room empties out
isn’t weakness
it’s the weight of knowing
you don’t need the noise
to prove you’re alive.

You let the storm
pass right through you
like a screen door
in an old summer house
letting in the breeze
but keeping the shape of you
intact.

And when they ask how
say nothing.
The quiet
is already speaking.

You're counting the cracks in the ceiling.
Every breath feels like borrowed time,
but the clock still ticks.
THEY SAY grief is loud,
yet yours hums,
quiet as a fridge,
steady,
unnoticed until the power cuts.
You trace the same circle on your knee,
again,
again,
and the shape doesn’t mean anything.
Nobody warns you about this,
this weight that isn’t heavy,
just... there.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
The porch light stayed on all summer.
We never turned it off,
just watched moths circle,
soft and reckless.
Some evenings feel like stolen time,
not borrowed,
not returned.
The air hums different when you're near,
less like breathing,
more like remembering how.
I keep the key,
not to unlock anything,
just to hold the weight.

Hafiz whispered
like dandelions in wind
we scatter seeds
not to keep
but to gift the sky.

you cling to what was never yours
a borrowed coat
a name on a lease
a shadow that walks beside you.

let the wind take it
it was never heavy
until you carried it alone.
watch it rise
like dust in sunlight

An old farmer once whispered
roots don’t ask the storm for mercy
they bend
then stand again
taller than before.

You’ll know them by the way
their hands steady the ladder
while you climb
not by the height they promise.
The earth remembers every step
even the ones you take alone.
Stay for the ones
who leave the light on.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
You’re carrying the grocery bags uphill again.
One handle snaps.
Oranges roll into the gutter.
You laugh,
but it’s the kind that cracks.
I watch from the porch,
and suddenly the hill isn’t steep.
The bags weigh nothing in my hands.
We don’t talk about the climb.
The sidewalk just feels shorter.
The oranges taste sweeter.
Send this to them.

the tide once whispered
across the wet stones
you are not the storm
nor the shelter
just the space between

and i realized
i’ve been building walls
when all i needed
was to let the wind pass through
you are my calm
the quiet that breathes
before the next wave

Missing you is a haze
we just
linger in memories
stopped being present
in each other's lives slowly
and somewhere between then and now we lost our rhythm
STILL we reach
for the ghost of you
NEW paths unfold
but the ache remains
in the hollow of my chest

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone You Should Not
scripts.script:
Some memories taste like stolen fruit.
Too sweet,
but you eat them anyway.
The first bite,
quiet betrayal,
then the slow rot in your pocket.
Nobody warns you
about the weight of what you can’t return.
You leave it on the windowsill,
hoping the sun will take it,
but dawn only makes it heavier.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
not boiling,
just waiting.
The scar on your wrist
isn’t a mistake—
it’s a compass.
Some nights,
it points north,
some nights,
it points to the version of you
who didn’t flinch.
The pain isn’t the enemy,
just the echo
of something trying to grow.
Let it.

It’s not surrender. Not really.
A river doesn’t fight the rock,
but the rock remembers the water’s name.
You’ve stopped pushing,
and the door swings open on its own.
The hinge creaks,
soft as a secret told at dawn.
You’re not wiser,
just softer in the places that used to ache.
The world gives what it gives,
and you’re learning to receive.
Not with hands,
with empty palms.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
How do you name the weight
that settles in your pockets
when the words won’t climb out?
Not heavy,
just there—
like river stones you’ve carried
since you were twelve.
Some secrets don’t scream,
they hum,
low and steady,
under every conversation.
I left them on the windowsill
where the light touches first.
Let them stay.

There's a kind of guilt that doesn't ache,
just lingers like a half-tied knot.
I saved your number,
not for hope,
but because some doors
should stay slightly ajar.
The screen glows,
fingers hover,
never press call,
just trace the digits
like a secret I can't bury.
Some ghosts don't haunt,
they just visit in quiet code.
I won't delete you,
I'll let you fade
into the background noise.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
You're tracing circles on my wrist,
like constellations no one named.
I don't need the sky explained,
just the way your thumb pauses
before it moves again.
We could be two spoons in a drawer,
quietly holding the shape of each other,
no clinking,
no hurry.
The air between us
learns the weight of your breath,
and I,
I learn to be light enough
to let you carry me without noticing.
send this to them.

You're tracing the cracks in your bedroom ceiling.
A spider weaves her world above you,
steady,
patient.
She doesn't ask the walls to change shape.
The corners know her name.
You press your palm to the cool glass,
watch your breath fog the night.
It doesn't stay,
but it was yours for a moment.
The streetlight hums a lullaby.
You hum back,
quietly,
until the melody belongs to you.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
The way your fingers trace the rim,
of an empty glass,
like you’re reading braille off yesterday’s news.
Somewhere between hunger,
and forgetting you haven’t eaten.
Your shadow stretches,
longer than the afternoon,
but refuses to let go of the chair.
I count the cracks in the ceiling,
while you count the steps,
to a door that isn’t there.
And when the light shifts,
you don’t flinch,
just tilt your head,
like the world owes you an answer.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
You trace the cracks in the ceiling.
Like constellations only we know,
I memorize the way your fingers hesitate.
Not afraid,
just savoring the weight of what comes next.
Your laugh is a pocket of air in stale rooms,
something small I carry between my ribs.
I don’t mean forever,
I mean this breath,
this quiet click of the lamp turning off,
your shoulder against mine like a question I don’t answer.
Stay. Just like this.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
I still pack their favorite sweater
in the suitcase every trip
just in case the road brings them home again
The zipper sticks at the same corner
a memory I can’t force shut
Some nights I leave it open
like an invitation
but the wind only carries dust
not their voice
I won’t close it completely
not yet
let the moths decide.

The last text you sent was still unsent.
It flickers like a moth against the screen,
soft wings brushing glass.
They read it three times,
but never saw the way your fingers trembled
while typing each letter.
WORDS don't always land where we aim.
Sometimes they scatter like seeds in the wind,
and grow into something wild,
something unrecognizable.
You keep the draft,
quiet proof of a truth they'll never understand.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
Tuesday, 2:17pm. The café hums.
Your name slips through my lips,
just a breath,
like sugar dissolving in tea.
I stir too long,
watching the swirl,
not tasting,
just waiting for the sweetness to settle.
Some words,
I write them down,
then tear the page,
not from anger,
but from knowing,
the ink was never yours to keep.

It doesn't feel empty. 
   Slowly 
   the weight of your presence settles 
   in a forgotten attic 
   where trunks are filled 
   with the softness of our moments 
   BUT the dust that gathers 
   on these memories is mine 
   to tend and gently brush away 
   and in this quiet ritual 
   I find a strange solace 
   in the folds of your abandoned scarf 
   send this to Quietlyy.

Happiness isn’t a place you find,
it’s a shadow you’ve always cast.
The sun moves,
and so do you,
but the shape never leaves.
Some days it stretches long,
tall as the trees,
other days it curls tight,
a secret tucked in your pocket.
The ancient truth?
You were never chasing light,
just learning to stand still
inside the dark you carry.

YOU LEFT THE LIGHT ON
in the kitchen
again
like I’d walk through that door
any second
I stopped checking the clock
stopped setting two plates
stopped pretending the couch
wasn’t just mine now
SOMEWHERE
between the last text
and the first gray hair
you became
a name I don’t say out loud
anymore

Home isn’t walls. Not really.
It’s the way the kettle hums,
just before it boils,
warm and patient,
like someone who knows you’re coming.
The sink is full of dishes,
but the water runs clear,
and for once,
you don’t mind the weight.
Some rooms remember your shape,
even when you forget.
You leave,
but the door never locks behind you.
It just waits.

There's a quiet like dandelion seeds.
They land,
soft,
on your palm.
You know they won't stay,
but you cup them anyway.
The wind takes them,
not cruel,
just honest.
Some things are meant to be held,
then let go.
The gift isn't the keeping.
It's the moment you chose to open your hands.
send this to them.

There's a quiet like a held breath
before the laugh escapes.
The way your fingers trace the rim of a cup,
unaware I'm memorizing the shape of your pause.
I built this smile from old receipts,
ink fading with every thank you,
and the corners curl when no one's looking.
Your name fits in the spaces between my teeth
where I hide the words I won't say out loud.
The clock hums,
but time moves differently near you—
slower,
softer,
like snow settling on a windowsill.
I let you see the version that knows how to glow,
while the real me
counts the cracks in the ceiling.
send this to them.
visual_keywords: a smile reflected in a spoon

scripts.script:
There's a kind of ache that lingers
like a half-tied knot.
Your fingers keep pulling,
but the loop never closes.
I trace the same frayed edges,
night after night,
wondering if you notice
how carefully I hold the rope.
The weight doesn't lessen,
just settles into my bones.
Maybe some knots aren't meant to be untangled.
send this to them.

The old gardener whispered
some wounds
never break the skin.
They bloom inward
like roots in the dark.

You walk through rooms
carrying a weight
no one names.
The body remembers
what the mouth won’t say.

Let the ache
rest like a stone
in your pocket.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
An old gardener whispered
roots don’t ask for light
they reach anyway
toward what they need
without knowing why.

I think that’s living —
not the bloom
the stubborn underground stretch
when no one’s watching.
Existing is just waiting
for the rain to stop.
Maybe the dirt
is enough.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone You Should Not
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the hours,
they pool like rainwater in my shoes.
Every puddle reflects your shadow,
but the sidewalk stays empty.
I trace your initials on fogged glass,
the letters disappear before I finish.
Some days,
the ache feels like a borrowed coat,
too big,
too heavy,
I wear it anyway.
The radiator hums your favorite song,
I pretend it's you humming back.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Last Text You Never Sent
scripts.script:
The cursor blinked
once
then twice
like a streetlight about to die.
I typed your name
and the whole screen
held its breath.
YOU WERE GONE
before the first word
landed.
Still,
I saved it
like a ticket stub
to a show
that never opened.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
You hand me the salt
like it’s a secret
I take it
like I’m stealing something.

THE AIR SHIFTS
just enough
to make the curtains
remember they’re alive.

We don’t say a word
but the room hums
with all the things
we’re not naming.

And suddenly
even the clock
feels like it’s holding its breath.

scripts.topic: When Love Feels Like Coming Home
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the steps
to your door.
The porch light hums,
soft orange like the last sip of tea.
I never told you how the floorboards creak
in the same rhythm as my old clock,
ticking your name.
Your voice,
a blanket draped over my shoulders
before I knew I was cold.
The way you leave the window open
just enough for the breeze to visit.
I carry it everywhere,
this quiet shape of belonging.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
How do you measure the weight
Of shoulders that never drop the load?
The same hands that mend every tear
slowly forget how to ask for help,
not because you don’t need it,
but because you’ve learned
Some people only stay for the calm.
The storm in you
becomes a quiet hum,
a background noise they sleep through,
and you,
You become the lighthouse
that keeps burning,
Even when no one’s looking.

Some friendships are like old bridges.
They creak under the weight of years,
but still hold every memory we walked across.
You were never just my favorite,
just the one who knew where I kept my secrets.
The laughter,
slowly turned to comfortable quiet,
not because we ran out of things to say,
but because we didn’t need to fill the space.
Some people,
we don’t lose them to distance,
we lose them to time,
and that’s okay.
You’re more than my favorite person.
You’re the reason I know how to let go.

There's a kind of wonder that fits in your palm.
A single raindrop on the window,
holding the whole sky inside.
It doesn't ask to be noticed,
just lingers,
and the world feels softer for it.
Your laugh does that too—
unfolds like a paper crane,
small,
but suddenly the room is lighter.
I keep these moments in my pockets,
where they warm my hands.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You're folding laundry like it's sacred,
just the way your mother taught you.
The sleeves align,
perfect creases,
but your eyes drift
to the window where the streetlight hums.
Some nights,
you let the wrinkles stay,
and I see the weight you carry
when no one's looking.
You smooth the collar,
slowly,
like you're tucking in a memory.
I won't interrupt.

You don’t need wings.
Not anymore.
The sky was always yours,
just…
the cage door stayed open,
and you never noticed.
Some days,
the wind still asks for your name,
but you answer differently now.
Not lighter.
Not heavier.
Just…
true.
The ground holds you,
and that’s enough.

Memories of you linger
we just
faded out
of each other's lives slowly
STILL we wonder
what could have been if
FATE had brought us closer
instead of apart
we just
ghosts of a what-if
still searching
for a glimpse of you

THE CLOCK hums
like a fridge full of ghosts
I COUNT the cracks in the ceiling
one for every version of me
you’d recognize
The fan spins
but the air stays thick
I REWRITE the conversation
word for word
until it sounds like someone else’s
YOU NEVER SAID that
I DID
in the dark
where no one checks the receipts

The porch light stayed on all night.
A single bulb
burning just for you.
I didn’t notice the way
your shadow stretched longer
than the driveway,
or how the crickets paused
when you stepped closer.
The screen door didn’t creak,
but the air did.
Something small and electric
clicked inside my ribs.
Send this to them.

Home isn’t four walls,
just the way the light
bends through dust at 3 PM,
soft as a promise kept.
Some rooms remember your shape,
even when you don’t.
The floorboards sigh,
not in complaint,
but like an old dog settling back
against your ankles.
You don’t own it—
you’re just borrowed time
between the cracks.

There's a gratitude that tastes like borrowed sugar.
You stir it into their coffee,
watch it dissolve without thanks.
The cup stays warm,
but your fingers go cold.
Some people wear you like a favorite sweater,
forgetting the hands that wove you.
You unravel thread by thread,
and still they ask for more.
The stitches hold,
but the shape is no longer yours.
Leave them to the chill.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
YOU SLIDE YOUR HAND
across the table
like a shadow
settling over cracks in the wood.
I forget
how heavy my own bones feel
until you do that.
THE KETTLE WHISPERS
but the steam
carries your voice instead—
soft as the way
you tuck the blanket
around my shoulders
before I even shiver.
visual_keywords: steam curling from a chipped mug
a blanket draped over slumped shoulders

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
I leave my coffee half-drunk on your doorstep
cold
before dawn
because I can’t say the words
YOU WALK RIGHT PAST
like I’m invisible
like the steam that rises
then vanishes
into the morning air
I COUNT THE STEPS
between us
and pretend it’s a game
I’m winning.

THE TEARS START
like a faucet no one turned on.
I check the pipes,
check the sky,
but the water keeps coming.
Maybe it’s the way the light slants
through the blinds at 3:17,
or the way my coffee tastes
of yesterday’s rain.
I don’t know.
I just sit
and let it pool
on the table.
visual_keywords: a half-empty mug with a single tear-shaped stain

scripts.script:
You're tracing the cracks in the ceiling again.
Like rivers deciding their own paths,
you let your fingers wander,
not because they know the way,
but because the pressure to choose
is lighter than the weight of choosing wrong.
Some doors creak open only when you stop pushing,
and the draft that slips through
carries the scent of rain on warm pavement.
You exhale.
The room doesn't rush to fill the space.
Send this to them.

I FORGOT MY NAME
in the mirror
this morning
it slipped
like a sock off a wet foot
I called out
but the echo
was someone else’s voice
I kept walking
past the bakery
where I used to buy bread
and the baker
didn’t even look up
SOMEWHERE
between the cracks
I left pieces
no one noticed
until the sidewalk
ended
and I wasn’t there

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
The body is a sketchbook,
never signed,
never closed.
Every scar,
every freckle,
a question mark,
left hanging.
Some days,
the pencil trembles,
but the page
stays open.
And the wind,
unafraid,
turns the corners.

scripts.topic: Loving Someone Who Can't Love Back
scripts.script:
YOU LEFT YOUR SHADOW
on my bedroom wall
it flickers
when the streetlight hums
I trace the edges
with my fingers
trying to remember
how it felt
to be seen
not just noticed
not just wanted
but known
and the shadow stays

I kept the light on
all night
just to watch you
breathe in the dark,
and suddenly
the walls knew your name
better than I did.
THE AIR SHIFTED
when you laughed,
carried your voice
like a secret it couldn’t keep.
Now the room
feels too big
without your shadow
leaning against mine.

It’s not pain.
Not the way you think.
The cracks don’t break you—
they let the light in,
slowly,
like roots through stone.
You don’t bend,
you grow
in the shape of what tried to kill you.
Some scars aren’t wounds,
they’re proof you learned to breathe underwater.
The weight doesn’t disappear—
you just carry it differently now.

scripts.topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
scripts.script:
You were my first wrong answer
I circled
then erased
without telling anyone.
The desk still has the ghost
of your name in pencil,
but the test changed.
I memorized new questions
while you practiced
old ones.
Somewhere in the margins
we both stopped
crossing out the same mistakes.

scripts.topic: I Didn't Know Love Could Feel This Soft
scripts.script:
I thought it would be sharp
like winter air
but your hands
unfolded me
slow as morning
before the kettle sings
I didn’t know
skin could hold this much light
that a voice
could be a blanket
pulled over my shoulders
I still reach for it
in the dark

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
It’s not sadness. It’s weather.
The windshield fogs like a memory half-remembered,
streaks of salt mapping something deeper than tears.
You press your palm to the cold glass,
leave a print that says I was here,
but the rain blurs it before you can.
HEADLIGHTS cut through the dark,
revealing nothing but your own reflection,
and for a second,
you don’t recognize the face.
The radio hums a song you’ve never heard,
but it knows the words to this moment.
Send this to them.

The river doesn’t ask for a name,
just flows,
carving patience into stone.
Some mornings,
you wake up lighter,
not because the weight is gone,
but because you learned to float.
The current doesn’t fight,
it just takes you,
and for once,
you let it.

scripts.topic: When You Delete the Message Before Sending It
scripts.script:
It wasn’t a mistake. Just gravity.
The words hung,
too heavy for the screen’s glow.
You watched them fall,
like leaves refusing the branch.
Not regret,
just the quiet pull of something unsaid.
The delete button,
a tiny black hole.
And now the draft folder
breathes lighter,
as if it knew all along.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
How do you name the shadow
that wears your face?
It doesn’t scream,
just hums a lullaby of lack
in the back of your throat.
The mirror flickers,
and suddenly you’re smaller,
just a smudge on the glass.
Some days you paint over it—
bright strokes of busy hands,
but the outline bleeds through.
What if it’s not a lie?
What if it’s just
an old coat you keep putting on?

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
You don’t remember. But I do.
The way your laugh
folded into my collarbone like a secret.
Not loud.
Just there.
And now every morning,
the coffee steams,
but the kitchen remembers your hands.
I trace the ghost of your thumbprint
on the sugar jar.
Some things don’t leave.
They just learn to hide.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
The toaster pops at 7:03.
Every morning
but today it sounds like applause.
You butter the bread
and wonder who’s clapping.
Some moments don’t belong to the clock
just to the way your fingers tremble
while spreading jam.
The kitchen light hums
but it’s not the bulb—
it’s the echo of a laugh
still warm in the air.
You eat standing up.
The crumbs taste different.

I stopped waiting for permission
to take up space
The room still hums with echoes
of their voices
but I unclenched my fists
and let the light in
Some days I forget
I’m allowed to stay
The mirror holds my gaze longer now
not searching for approval
just learning the shape of my own name
I belong to myself
finally.

Some nights I trace the constellations,
just to name the spaces between,
where your voice would fit,
like a missing puzzle piece,
not lost,
just waiting.
The ceiling cracks hold galaxies,
I swear I see your shadow,
stretching,
not to touch,
but to remind me,
you’re still there.

You don’t want to keep it.
Not really.
The way morning light lingers
on the rim of your coffee cup,
then slips away without asking.
That’s the kind of thing you hoard—
not gold,
not promises,
just the quiet ache of something
beautiful enough to let go.
It doesn’t stay.
It shouldn’t.
Send this to them.

You don’t cry. Not like this.
The mirror only shows the edges,
softly lit,
but the cracks hum beneath.
Some faces hold a whole season—
autumn in the eyes,
winter in the teeth.
I learned to laugh with my back turned,
so the wind carries the truth away.
You’ll never see the roots,
just the leaves I let you touch.
I am not hiding.
I am the soil that grows in silence.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
We are cartographers of coincidence,
marking x’s on moments,
not where we landed,
but where we decided they mattered.
Every shadow,
every flicker of streetlight,
becomes a constellation,
stitched from the thread of attention.
Some people,
walk through the same rain,
and call it weather.
We call it baptism,
we call it proof,
we call it whatever keeps us looking up.

scripts.topic: Missing a Person Who Is Still Here
scripts.script:
Some days I forget to listen,
just hear the echo of your laugh,
warm as sunlight through half-drawn blinds.
The kitchen hums,
but the kettle never boils right,
missing the way you stirred the sugar in.
I trace your absence like a shadow,
long and familiar,
clinging to the edges of every room.
You’re still here,
just not in the places I reach.

SLOWLY we learn                      
just by listening                 
to the spaces between words      
FINDING comfort in stillness   
we begin to hear ourselves think 
and somewhere in the quiet hours 
still we search for a gentle truth 
GENTLY unfolding like a mist   
that brings us to our knees     
softly

You’re tightening the laces.
One loop.
Then another.
Not for speed,
just to stand taller.
The ground hasn’t changed,
but your shadow stretches longer now.
Some days,
you forget to apologize
for taking up space.
The mirror still flickers,
but you don’t flinch.
Walk away.
Not running.
Choosing.
visual_keywords: empty street with one lit window

scripts.script:
How do you name the weight
that sits behind your thumbs
not heavy
just present
like a shadow you can’t shake
You type the words
then watch them dissolve
not erased
just quietly withdrawn
Some truths don’t need sending
they only need to be felt
once.
send this to them.

I stopped counting the mornings
like loose change in my pocket.
Your name clinks against my ribs,
soft as the hinge on my grandmother’s jewelry box.
I never told you how the light
spills through the gap in the curtains,
pooling on the floor like spilled milk.
We step around it,
careful not to disturb the gold.
The clock hums,
a lullaby for the hours we’ve borrowed.
I still leave the porch light on,
not for you,
but for the version of me that stayed.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
There’s a hunger that isn’t for food
it gnaws at the edges of your ribs
not for what you eat
but for what you’ve swallowed whole
Some days you’re a shadow
cast by someone else’s light
and the wind doesn’t even notice you’re there
But living
living is the crack in the sidewalk
where a single weed pushes through
not defiant
just stubbornly alive
You don’t have to bloom
to matter.

The porch light stays on all night.
I don’t remember leaving it,
just the way it glows
soft on empty steps.
Some shadows don’t need names,
they sit beside you
until you stop flinching.
The keys jingle less now,
not from forgetting,
but from letting go.
The door closes
without a sound.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I don’t need the light on
to know
where your shoulder ends
and the dark begins.
WE WERE NEVER LOST
just drifting
in the same slow orbit
around something brighter.
The coffee cup left
on the counter
holds the shape of your grip
long after you’ve gone.
I trace it
like a constellation
that only I can see.

WE SHARED the same streetlights
but
you turned left
I kept walking
past the corner store
where we used to split a soda
now the neon hums
to an empty booth
and the sidewalk cracks
remember how we laughed
at nothing
now we step over them
like strangers
doing time

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
the old farmer said
rain doesn’t ask permission
it just comes
softens the hard earth
until even rocks remember how to bend.

you show up like that
not with answers
just a steady hand on the wheel
when the road turns to mud
and the headlights blur.

you don’t fix the storm
you teach the trees to sway.

You leave the porch light on
all night
just in case
I decide to swing by
again.

I WATCH IT FLICKER
from the bus stop
three blocks down
where the bench still holds
my shape.

The bulb hums
but never burns out
and I wonder
if it’s waiting
or just tired.

scripts.topic: When Love Feels Like Coming Home
scripts.script:
The door swings wide,
unlocked by memory.
Not a key,
just a feeling—
warm light spilling,
soft as old cotton.
Some nights,
I swear I hear the floorboards sigh,
remembering the weight of us.
The kettle hums,
not boiling,
just waiting.
And I step in,
shaking off the cold,
like a coat I never meant to wear.

You’re not empty. Just waiting.
The room exhales around you,
breathing in the weight of what hasn’t happened yet.
A single candle flickers,
but the wax doesn’t melt—
It pools,
thick and patient,
like the quiet before a name is spoken.
Your hands know the shape of stillness,
how to hold what hasn’t asked to be held.
The night leans in,
whispering,
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
I carry the scar like a compass.
Needle always points to the moment,
not north.
The ache taught me how to read the sky,
clouds spelling your name in disappearing ink.
I trace the edges,
but the ink never stays.
Some lessons only bloom in the dark,
petals unfolding where the light won’t go.
The wound remembers what my hands forgot.
I press my palm to the old shape,
and the world tilts just enough to see you.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Delete the Message Before Sending It
scripts.script:
I stopped the ink before it bled,
just a flicker on the screen,
then darkness swallowed every word
like a tide pulling back secrets.
The cursor blinked,
mocking the weight of what I almost said,
not regret,
just the quiet ache of unsent apologies.
Some truths float,
buoyant,
but never break the surface.
I let them drown in drafts,
and the ocean keeps my name.

You're tracing circles on my palm.
like a compass
that always points back to morning,
not north.
The way you laugh at my jokes
before I finish telling them—
that's the kind of gravity
I want to orbit.
Some people are just weather,
passing through without changing the forecast.
But you?
You're the kind of light
that makes shadows worth keeping.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
The porch light stays on all night.
I count the moths,
but they’re not yours to keep.
SLOWLY MY SHOULDERS LEARN
to hold the weight of every hug,
while my ribs crack under the pressure.
The houseplant you gave me
thrives in the corner,
its leaves stretching toward a sun it can’t reach.
I water it every morning,
pretending I don’t see the roots rotting.
The world calls me steady.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
Grief isn’t a weight you carry.
It’s the shape of the space left behind,
cool to the touch,
like a glass that used to hold something sweet.
Some mornings,
you pour yourself in,
and it fits,
not because you’re whole,
but because the cracks learned to hold light.
The body remembers,
but it also forgets,
slowly,
like a tide pulling back,
leaving only the salt on your skin.
You don’t heal,
you adapt,
and one day,
you realize the empty space
is just another kind of full.

I didn’t know I was lost
until
you
pulled the stars down
to light my name
on the sidewalk.
WE WERE NEVER HERE BEFORE
just two shadows
learning how to cast light
instead of shade.
The streetlamps hummed our secret
and for a second
I believed in forever
like a child believes in snow.

You are not the storm.
Not even the wind.
You're the stillness between raindrops,
the pause before breath becomes sound.
I didn't know quiet could be this alive,
this full of unseen wings.
The way you hold space,
like a bowl of warm light,
unspilled, unshaken.
I lean into you,
and the world stops trying to explain itself.
send this to them.

You don’t regret them. Just the version of you
that believed they’d stay.
The suitcase still sits by the door,
half-packed with things you’ll never need.
Rain taps the window like a reminder,
soft but insistent.
And the coffee cup you shared
still smells like their cologne—
not bitter, just... lingering.
Some mistakes feel like home
until you realize the door was never locked.
Send this to them.

2am. The radiator hums low.
I press my palm to the wall,
not testing,
just remembering the warmth.
Some rooms hold more than furniture—
they keep the shape of shoulders,
the echo of a laugh that hasn’t left.
I don’t need to name it safe,
just let the dark wrap around me
like a coat I never take off.
The door stays closed,
and the world outside forgets I’m here.

There's a kind of brightness that dims.
The way your name sits on my tongue,
like a sugar cube dissolving
too slow to taste sweet.
I laugh,
but the sound is a paper crane
folded from a grocery list—
all the things we needed,
all the things I forgot to buy.
The mirror shows me smiling,
and I wonder
who's hiding behind the glass.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
You're sitting in the stillness. 
Slowly 
Your fingers brush against mine, 
Gently, 
A whispered promise of presence. 
But the darkness outside doesn't scare me, 
And the city lights blur into a haze, 
Softly enveloping us. 
In this quiet, I find my footing, 
NEW Dawns arrive with your patient hands, 
And the world, 
Slowly, 
Fades into the background. 
Send this to Quietlyy.

I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling.
They multiply like the versions of you I invent,
soft echoes in the dark.
Every word we didn’t say
becomes a shadow puppet on the wall,
dancing without permission.
The sheets remember the shape of your absence,
cool where your warmth should be.
I trace the edges of the pillow,
pretending it’s your collarbone.
The night stretches,
and I let it.
Send this to them.

Happiness isn’t a place you arrive.
It’s the dust on your shoes
from walking the same path twice,
not lost,
just circling back
to see what you missed.
Some mornings,
the light hits the wall differently,
and you realize
you’ve been holding your breath
for years.
Breathe.
The truth was always there,
waiting in the corners
of ordinary days.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like unopened mail.
I keep the envelopes in my top drawer,
softly yellowing,
still sealed.
The ink never smudged,
but the weight of what's inside
presses against my ribs.
I trace your name on the front,
count the stamps like constellations.
Some words don't need air,
they just need to exist.
Send this to them.

The old gardener whispered
roots remember
what branches forget.

they twist through seasons
without asking permission.

you think you chose the soil
but the soil chose you first.
its patience is deeper
than your longest goodbye.

the tree does not apologize
for growing where it was planted.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the seconds
between your glances
like a child measuring rain
each drop a promise
that never lands
The way you look at me
isn’t kindness
it’s cartography
mapping the parts of myself
i didn’t know were missing
until your eyes found them
I won’t ask you to stay
just let me memorize the shape of your attention
before it fades.

scripts.topic: The Family Member Who Hurt You Most
scripts.script:
YOU TAUGHT ME HOW TO HOLD A KNIFE
not for cutting bread
for cutting ties
and I still
flinch
when the blade catches light
YOUR WORDS WERE NEVER SHARP ENOUGH
to kill
just enough to make me bleed slow
in the backseat of your car
while you hummed along to the radio
I COUNTED THE MILES
between your house and mine
now I count the years
between your voice
and my name
visual_keywords: dust on a forgotten record player

scripts.topic: Being Tired in Ways Sleep Cannot Fix
scripts.script:
This exhaustion isn’t a yawn,
it’s a slow unraveling.
The spine of a book left open too long,
binding cracked,
pages curling at the edges.
Some mornings the weight isn’t in your bones,
but in the air itself,
thick as ink that won’t dry.
You press your palm to the window,
just to feel something solid,
but the glass is colder than you expected,
and the sky outside,
just another blank page.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
You don’t believe in forever.
Not like this.
The way they trace your name
in the steam on morning coffee,
not a grand gesture,
just habit,
soft as the way dawn
drips through the blinds.
Some choices aren’t fireworks,
they’re the quiet click
of a key turning,
again,
in the same old lock.
You’re not saved.
You’re chosen,
day after ordinary day,
and that’s the miracle.

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
You turn the key. The engine hums alive.
Rain taps the windshield like Morse code from ghosts.
Your hands grip the wheel,
not steering,
just holding on.
The dashboard glows,
soft orange numbers counting nothing.
Your breath fogs the glass,
writing secrets the rearview won’t keep.
The radio plays a song you used to know,
now just static and static and static.
You’re not sad.
You’re just here,
letting the car remember for you.

scripts.topic: Loving Someone Who Can't Love Back
scripts.script:
You don’t need them. Not like this.
A houseplant wilting in the corner,
reaching for light it can’t see.
I water you anyway.
The leaves curl inward,
softly refusing the sun.
I talk to the soil,
whispering names of storms that never came.
You grow. Just not toward me.
I keep the blinds open.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
You reach for my hand
in the dark
but the shape of your fingers
has already slipped
through mine like mist
I count your breaths
one
two
three
and wonder how many more
before the morning takes them
YOU STILL SMELL LIKE HOME
but the door
won’t stay open

You don’t own yourself yet. 
But the room is learning your name.
A single lamp hums low,
casting shadows that bend to your shape.
They don’t ask where you’ve been.
The walls remember every step,
and the floorboards sigh,
settling under your weight like an old promise.
You are not lost here.
Just uncharted,
like a river carving its own bed.
The mirror doesn’t judge.
It only holds your gaze,
until you recognize the stranger.
send this to yourself.

The porch light stays on all night.
I pretend it’s for me,
but it’s just waiting for your shadow
to stretch across the driveway again.
Some nights I catch myself
counting the steps from the curb to the door,
