like a ritual I can’t unlearn.
The key still fits,
but the lock feels different now.
I don’t turn it.
Just hold it there,
warm in my palm,
like a secret I’m not ready to tell.
The house breathes without you.

The clock hands don’t move,
just hum,
like a fridge in an empty kitchen.
Some nights
they forget to tick,
and the dark gets heavier,
not from absence,
but from the weight of what’s unsaid.
The walls remember every argument,
every laugh,
every door left unopened.
They breathe,
slow and steady,
until you learn to listen.

scripts.topic: I Didn't Know Love Could Feel This Soft
scripts.script:
There's a tenderness that hums,
like the fridge at 3 a.m.
No one hears it,
but it keeps everything fresh.
Your touch,
a quiet current
pulling the sheets into place.
I didn't know bodies could settle
like dust after rain,
soft,
unnoticed,
perfectly there.
send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered
not the loudest voice
but the one that listens
earns the mountain's trust.

you’ve spent years
building altars to others’ names,
while your own name
faded in the wind.

what if the revolution
isn’t a shout
but the moment
you stop apologizing
for taking up space.
visual_keywords: single lantern in a dark room
hands cupped around a candle flame

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the cracks in the ceiling again.
A spider weaves its web,
quiet architect of something delicate.
You call it luck.
I call it proof.
But the voice hums like static,
steady as the fridge light,
buzzing you’re not built to last.
The web trembles,
still holds.
You exhale.
The cracks don’t.
send this to yourself.

YOU TURNED THE PAGE
too fast
I didn’t finish reading
but the ink
bled through anyway
like a secret I wasn’t supposed to see
YOU SMILED
and the room tilted
just enough to notice
the way your shadow
stretched longer than mine
I knew then
we were standing in different light

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
The porch light stays on all night.
I didn’t ask it to.
but it flickers,
like a pulse I can’t control.
Some doors,
you don’t get to close,
they stay ajar,
letting in the cold you can’t stop.
The key,
though,
is still yours—
what you lock,
what you leave open,
what you walk away from.
Turn it slow.

You're tracing circles on my palm.
Like you're memorizing the shape of my bones,
not the skin.
I don’t flinch.
Even when your finger lingers too long,
I let the quiet weight of it
press into me—
not a promise,
just proof.
You don’t have to say it.
The way you stay
is enough.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I forget my name
when you walk in
just
breathe
and the room tilts
soft as a hammock in summer.
WE DON’T TOUCH
WE DON’T SPEAK
but the air hums
like a porch swing
creaking in the dark.
Your sleeve brushes mine
and suddenly
I remember how to stay.

The room was always white,
but you brought the rain.
Not the storm,
just the kind that blurs the window,
makes the world outside feel softer.
I stopped counting days,
started measuring time in droplets.
Somewhere between the first drop
and the last,
the walls learned to breathe.
Now the air hums,
not empty,
just different.

Hafiz knew this truth—
they will take what you lend
but never what you keep.

watch how the moon
never apologizes
for its light,
yet the night still holds it.

you are not a well
you are the sky
that decides what rain to catch.

some hands cup only thirst,
some hands cup only stars—
choose which one you feed.

scripts.topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
scripts.script:
You kept the light on
in the hallway
for years
I never asked
why
until the night
you forgot
and I stood there
counting shadows
like unopened letters
wondering
if you knew
I was home.

I waited
by the screen door
until the hinge creaked
its tired song
YOU NEVER SHOWED
not even a shadow
stretching down the hall
to say I’m sorry
I kept the light on
all summer
just in case
you changed your mind
still warm
in the dark

I stopped counting the steps.
The staircase creaks only for me,
and the echo learns my name.
Sometimes I leave the door unlocked,
not for company,
but so the wind can visit.
It brings the scent of rain on pavement,
slowly teaching me the shape of empty rooms.
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
pretending they’re constellations,
and I’m the only one who knows their stories.
The quiet isn’t empty.
It’s a bowl I’m learning to hold.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that has no mouth.
It gnaws at the edges of your ribs,
not for food,
but for the way light bends through leaves.
EXISTING is counting breaths,
LIVING is tasting the air after rain.
The clock hands don't move,
they dissolve into the hum of the fridge.
You don't notice the difference
until you catch yourself smiling at nothing.
The world tilts,
just enough to let the gold spill in.
Send this to them.

Shadows crept in
we just
stopped believing
in forever promises
and somewhere between then and NOW
we lost our grip
on the pieces of us
still trying to
find our way back
SLOWLY
we unraveled
and the ache remains

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the empty chairs,
The table set for two,
just me,
and the ghost of your excuses.
Some nights,
I still hear the clink of your fork,
but the plate is cold,
The candle never lit.
I won’t ask again,
The door stays open,
not for you,
but for the wind to carry the dust away.

There's a quiet like the first page of a notebook,
empty but already humming.
The pen hovers,
waiting for the courage to press down.
Some roads don't need signs,
they just know the way your shoes fit.
Trust isn't a sudden yes,
but the slow unclenching of your grip.
The map was never the journey,
only your hands tracing the edges.
Let the ink bleed,
let the pages fill.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Friends Who Only Call When They Need You
scripts.script:
There's a kind of ache
that wears your name like borrowed shoes.
They only fit
when the road gets rough,
soft soles padding their way to you.
The laces come undone,
and suddenly,
you're standing barefoot in the hallway,
watching them walk out
with the rain still on their jacket.
The door clicks shut.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
the old gardener whispered
some seeds never bloom
some roots twist underground
forever reaching
never breaking soil.

you call it failure
i call it quiet growth
what if the unfinished
is the only art we truly make
what if the cracks
are the light getting in
not the end
but the shape of becoming

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
a stranger once whispered
across the crowded room
the moon doesn’t ask the tide
to stay
yet it pulls anyway.

you trace their name
in steam on the bathroom mirror
knowing it will vanish
before the water cools.

what is heavier
a feather held in the hand
or one carried
for miles in the wind.

let the weight be yours alone
no need to name it.

the old gardener whispered
some roots tangle only for a season.
some branches bend
till the wind decides to let them rise.
you will carry the shape of their weight
long after the storm has passed.
but soil remembers every seed
that chose not to stay.
you are not the garden
you are the rain
that taught it how to grow without them.
let the empty rows
remind you what blooms next.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
How do you name the moment
when the light catches your eyelashes
and you don’t flinch—
just stare through the window
like the world outside is a story
you’ve already read,
but keep turning pages anyway.
I trace the shape of your shoulders,
the way they curve like a question
no one’s brave enough to ask.
You don’t know I’m here,
and that’s the only time
you look like home.

scripts.topic: When Healing Hurts More Than the Wound
scripts.script:
The scar tissue is thicker,
stretching,
pulling,
like a sail in the wrong wind.
It catches every gust,
but never lifts.
Some mornings,
the ache isn’t the cut—
it’s the stitches,
tugging,
reminding,
this is how you mend.

You're folding the blanket just so.
The way it tucks under your chin
like a promise no one else remembers.
I watch the corners align,
perfect creases holding the dark.
This is the safest place I know —
not a room,
not a bed,
but the exact second
your hands pause mid-air,
breathing me in without looking up.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
4:17 p.m. The blinds are half-drawn.
Your eyes
are a compass I can’t calibrate,
pointing everywhere
but never true north.
I’ve memorized the tilt,
the way your lashes cast shadows
like questions I’m too afraid to answer.
Some afternoons,
I let myself believe
you’re searching for something in me.
But the truth is quieter—
just two people
standing in the same light,
seeing completely different skies.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that has no plate,
no fork,
just the shape of your absence
in the chair across from me.
The coffee still steams,
but the cup feels lighter,
like you took a sip
without touching it.
Some spaces don't need distance
to ache,
just the quiet weight
of wanting what's already here.
Send this to them.

You don’t resist. That’s the secret.
The river doesn’t beg the stone to smooth,
just flows
around the edges you thought were sharp.
Some things soften only when you stop trying,
like a door that swings open
when your hands aren’t clenched.
The quietest power is knowing
what to leave untouched,
what to let settle
like dust in sunlight.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the crack in the ceiling again.
Like a river you refuse to dam.
I brought no tools,
just the quiet weight of my palm
against your ribs.
You called it surrender.
I called it choosing to stay.
The house still creaks,
the pipes still hum,
but the silence between us
isn’t empty—
it’s full of all the things
we never had to fix.
send this to them.

Some nights,
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
like rivers on a dry tongue.
They don’t lead anywhere,
just split,
and split again,
until the dark is a delta of questions.
The bed’s too wide,
but I don’t move,
just let the weight of absence
press me into the shape of a comma,
waiting for a sentence that never ends.

It’s not trust. Not like that.
Your name fits in my palm
like a pebble I found by the river,
smooth and unremarkable,
but I keep it anyway.
The way you laugh at nothing
is the only sound I want to memorize,
not for the melody,
but for the way it settles in my ribs
like the first light through curtains,
soft and unasked for.
I don’t need you to stay.
I just need you to exist
somewhere near.
send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered
let go of the branch
and the wind will carry you
not to fall
but to fly.

I used to grip every leaf
afraid the sky would forget my name.
Now I watch the clouds rearrange themselves
without my permission.
It’s not surrender
it’s the first real breath
after years of holding.

The horizon doesn’t ask for tickets.
visual_keywords: untied boat on a still lake

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
I carry the eggshells in my pockets.
They weigh nothing,
but the cracks sing every time I move.
I laugh,
and the sound is a porcelain bell
no one hears shatter inside.
THEY ASK HOW I STAY SO STRONG,
and I smile,
my teeth the last unbroken pieces.
The basket never empties,
just fills with lighter,
more fragile things.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
YOU LEARN TO FOLD IT
like a letter
kept in your back pocket
not to read
just to know it’s there
THE WEIGHT SHIFTS
with every step
each crease a little softer
until one day
it fits
inside the seam
where no one sees
and you forget
to check.

I stopped checking the clock at 3:17,
just the way shadows do when the sun gives up.
The porch light stayed on,
but the moths found brighter things.
I never told you
I counted their wings,
or how the quiet hum of the fridge
became my favorite lullaby.
Some nights,
the dark tastes like old tea—
bitter, but familiar.
I won’t pour it out.
The house still breathes,
but the air doesn’t miss you anymore.

There's a kind of weight
that doesn't show on scales,
just settles in your shoulders
like a coat you borrowed
for someone else's winter.
The sleeves are too long,
but you keep pulling them down,
covering their hands,
tucking them into pockets
that were never yours.
Some burdens feel like kindness
until your arms forget how to rest.
Let them carry their own cold.

I stopped counting the mornings
when your name tastes like fresh coffee.
The way you stir the sugar in,
slowly...
not too fast,
not too slow,
just enough to make it sweet.
I never told you
I memorized the rhythm of your spoon.
The clink against porcelain,
a lullaby for days I didn’t think I’d keep.
You are the quiet hum
before the world wakes up.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
We are gardeners of our own ghosts,
pulling weeds,
not to erase,
but to make room.
Some days,
the soil resists,
hard and unyielding,
but the hands that dig
know the worth of stubborn roots.
We choose what blooms here,
not by force,
but by the quiet persistence
of light through cracks.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the miles between us,
not because they don’t matter,
but because you’re already here,
carried in the weight of my pockets—
coins, a ticket stub, your laugh folded small.
Some days I pull it out,
just to feel the edges,
not to unfold,
not to explain,
just to know it’s real.
I won’t ask for more,
this quiet gravity is enough.
let them know.

You were a guest
in your own skin
for years
until the door
finally clicked shut behind you.
NO ONE TOLD YOU
it was okay
to stay.
You learned the shape
of your own shadow
then stepped into it
like a room
with the lights already on.
visual_keywords: the quiet hum of a refrigerator

scripts.script:
You’re folding the same shirt again.
Third time tonight.
The creases remember your hands,
but...
the fabric doesn’t.
Some changes are invisible
until the light hits them just right.
You used to iron at dawn,
now...
even the wrinkles feel like proof.
Not of time,
of you.
Staying.
Different.

The first time I knew wasn’t a moment.
It was a slow unraveling,
like a sweater pulled loose at the seam.
not from force,
just time and gravity,
thread by thread,
until the whole thing sagged,
and I stood there,
holding the weight of what used to fit,
wondering when it stopped,
but knowing,
I’d never wear it the same way again.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
The first light through the blinds,
not dawn,
just the streetlamp giving up.
I count the cracks in the ceiling,
like Morse code from another life.
Some nights,
the sheets still smell like your detergent,
not memory,
something real.
The clock ticks,
but it’s not time,
just habit wearing thin.
I reach for my phone,
not to call,
just to see your name,
still there,
still first.

Lao Tzu whispered
not walls or a roof
the weight of a bowl in your hands
warm broth rising
steam curling like a question you forgot to ask.

you’ve carried that bowl before
the way your fingers remember the curve
the way your breath steadies
before the first sip.

home is the pause
the unclenching
before the world rushes back in.
visual_keywords: steam rising from a clay bowl

scripts.script:
It’s not goodbye. It’s just geography.
The way the tide leaves shells,
then returns to claim them,
without apology.
You’ll find one in your pocket,
months from now,
still warm from the sun.
It doesn’t belong to you,
but you’ll keep it anyway.
The ocean doesn’t ask for souvenirs.
Neither did I.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
THE WAY YOUR LAUGHTER
starts
before the joke lands.
You tilt your head
just a little
when you’re listening.
I COUNT THE FRECKLES
on your left wrist
like constellations.
You hum
old songs under your breath
while tying your shoes.
I KNOW THE SHAPE
of your shadow
when the sun hits it right.
You leave crumbs
on the counter
like tiny breadcrumbs back.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
IT WHISPERS
while you tie your shoes
it’s the second guess
before the door clicks shut
YOU COULD STAY
just one more minute
one more rehearsal
one more coat of paint
it hums
beneath the applause
the handshake
the thank-you note
YOU SHOULD HAVE
said more
done less
been different
it lingers
like static
on a dead channel
waiting
for the next commercial break.

THE TEARS COME
like summer rain on a screen door
no one opened
no one asked for
just
steady
steady
I press my palm to the glass
fog blooms
around the shape of my hand
and for a second
everything
is softer
then it’s gone.

You are the hush
before the storm
I never saw coming.
I held my breath
and you were water
seeping through my fingers,
cool,
unhurried.
THE SKY REMEMBERS
how we used to lie there,
counting clouds
that dissolved
into nothing.

I stopped waiting for the door to creak open,
just stood there,
keys in my own palm,
The lock turned,
not with a click,
but with the weight of knowing,
Some thresholds aren’t meant to be crossed twice,
The hallway stretches,
empty and gold,
and I walk,
not toward,
not away,
just home.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
You’re rearranging the same three mugs,
again.
The one with the chip,
still your favorite,
just sits there,
collecting dust like a museum piece.
Some mornings,
you pour the coffee,
but the cup stays empty,
waiting for a hand that never reaches.
You laugh,
but the kettle’s whistle is hollow,
and the steam,
just fogs the window.
You were the backup plan,
the extra setting at the table,
quietly,
polishing the silverware.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
YOU SWALLOW THE WORDS
before they sting
like bitter pills
meant to heal.
I watch
the way your jaw unclenches
slowly
like a door left ajar
for the hundredth time.
The air doesn’t change
but it should.
You always let me back in
while I pretend
not to notice
you’re already halfway gone.

The porch light stays on all night.
Only for you.
A single bulb,
softly humming your name.
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk,
counting steps to your door.
The neighbor’s dog still wags,
but only when your shadow passes.
Even the mailbox waits,
letters addressed to both of us.
I leave the key under the mat,
knowing you’ll never use it.
The house exhales when you’re near.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Tired in Ways Sleep Cannot Fix
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
but the steam won’t warm this ache.
I’ve counted every crack in the ceiling,
Slowly,
they’ve become constellations of my fatigue.
Some nights,
the weight isn’t in my bones—
It’s in the air,
thick like syrup,
and I’m drowning,
not in water,
but in the quiet drag of minutes.
The clock’s hands,
steady and cruel,
know this tired isn’t sleep’s to fix.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
I am a shadow
stretching in the wrong direction,
leaning toward light I can’t touch.
Some days,
it feels like gravity,
this pull I didn’t ask for.
The way my name sounds
when it leaves your lips—
a question you’ll never answer.
I trace the edges of your absence,
like a blind man memorizing a face.
Not to keep,
just to know.

A traveler once whispered
across the dunes of time
words are footprints
left in the sand
before the tide returns.

you trace them again
fingers hovering
like clouds over a forgotten shore
wondering whose steps they were
or if they were ever yours at all.

the wind doesn’t answer
it only carries.

scripts.topic: Loving Someone Who Can't Love Back
scripts.script:
You’re watering a plant that won’t grow.
The soil stays dry,
no matter how gentle your hands.
Some people,
just don’t drink.
You whisper to the leaves,
but the roots never hear.
It’s not cruelty,
just a different kind of air.
You pour anyway,
because hope is a habit.
The pot stays empty.
Let them go.

scripts.topic: I Didn't Know Love Could Feel This Soft
scripts.script:
I thought tenderness was a myth
whispered
by poets
who never held
anything fragile enough.
YOUR HANDS
are the first thing
that ever learned me
without breaking.
I didn’t know
skin could memorize kindness
like a lullaby.
Now every room
feels too loud
without your breath
on my neck.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
The cut you can’t see
heals into a door,
swollen at the edges,
just waiting for the right key.
Some nights it creaks open,
revealing not darkness,
but a room full of stars
stitched from your own lost light.
You don’t step inside,
not yet,
just trace the constellations
with trembling fingers,
and realize the wound was never a hole—
it was a telescope.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I remember the weight of your keys
on the table
between us
like something we both pretended not to hear.
You’d reach
and for a second
my shoulder would lean in
not to touch
you
but the space your hand just left.
The radio hummed
old static
and I counted the cracks in the ceiling
until they blurred
into constellations
we never named.

Hafiz whispered
some souls are lanterns
lit from within
not by the wind
but by something older.

you’ll know them
when the room brightens
without a single candle lit,
when the weight of years
feels lighter on your shoulders.

stay for the glow
that doesn’t ask for matches.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone You Should Not
scripts.script:
You keep the porch light on for them.
Still warm,
but the bulb’s been dead for weeks.
Some nights,
you swear it flickers,
just enough to trick your chest.
The house knows better,
quietly rearranging its shadows
to make room for what won’t stay.
You press your palm to the window,
not to feel the cold,
but to prove the glass is real.
Send this to them.

A river doesn’t apologize
for carving its own path,
just hums against the banks,
soft but unshaken.
Some days you’ll be the current,
some days the stone,
but never the flood that drowns itself.
The hands that cup the water
aren’t trying to own it,
just hold it long enough
to remember the shape of letting go.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You tilt your head
just
like the old porch swing
creaking in summer rain.
The way your fingers trace
absent circles on your knee
reminds me
of my grandmother’s quilt
stitched with threads
she never named.
I could watch forever
but you turn
and the spell breaks—
I am caught.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the freckles on your collarbone.
They were constellations I memorized in the dark,
but the sky shifts when you turn over.
Your breath is a tide I can’t predict,
and I’ve learned to float instead of anchor.
Some nights I trace the veins in your wrist,
slowly like a river learning its own name.
I never told you how the light through your lashes
makes me believe in stained glass again.
You are the quietest kind of miracle,
and I keep it folded in my pocket like a secret ticket.
send this to them.

I stopped counting the miles
back to your doorstep
but the scent of rain on pavement
still pulls me home
Not the walls
or the street name
just the way your laugh
sits in the kitchen light
Some nights
I forget the address
and follow the echo instead
quietly
like a compass
made of memory.

When did the weight of your absence
stop crushing my ribs
and start sharpening my edges,
not as punishment
but as a whetstone for living?
I trace the cracks in the sidewalk
where your laughter once split the concrete,
and suddenly,
the world feels less like a place to endure
and more like a lesson I’m finally ready to learn.
Grief isn’t the ghost at the table anymore—
it’s the chair I pull up for myself,
quietly,
to keep the conversation going.

When did the sky stop being blue for you,
not because it changed,
but because you learned to name the clouds differently.
Some mornings,
you wake up,
and the air tastes like old birthday candles,
slowly melting into nothing.
The hands that once held yours,
now forget the shape of your fingers,
and you,
quietly,
let them.
It’s not sadness,
just the sound of a door,
closing on a room you’ll never enter again.

You’re folding the same shirt again.
Third time tonight.
The crease won’t hold,
but neither will you.
Some days,
acceptance isn’t a door you walk through—
it’s the window you stop boarding up.
Light spills in,
not golden,
just ordinary,
and you let it.
The weight you carried wasn’t yours to keep.
Put it down.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
Gravity pulls harder on some mornings.
The bed resists,
but your voice is the hinge
that lets the light in.
Not with answers,
just the quiet certainty
you’d carry half the weight
if I asked.
Some days,
that’s the only bridge
I need to cross.
You make the hard days easier.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in my spine
from carrying everyone else’s skies
The weight doesn’t bend me anymore
it just settles deeper
like river stones in winter
Some nights I press my palms to the ceiling
and push up
but the roof stays low
I won’t let them see the tremors
only the steady hands holding their world
I built this strength brick by brick
and now I’m the house
no one notices the foundation
until it starts to sink
let them know.
visual_keywords: shadow of a solitary figure under streetlight

scripts.script:
You're tracing the crack in the ceiling.
Like a river deciding its path,
you hesitate at the fork.
The bedsheet still holds the shape of yesterday,
but the pillow smells like fresh laundry now.
SOME DAYS,
you won't need a reason to stand up.
The morning light will find you mid-step,
unsteady but certain.
You'll leave the door unlocked,
just in case courage comes knocking.
Send this to yourself tomorrow.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
Hafiz knew this—

the one you miss
is the one holding your coat
while you stare at the rain.

what you ache for
isn’t absence
it’s the space between your ribs
the echo of a name
you keep swallowing.

you reach for them
like a child groping the dark
for a hand that’s already there—
warm, familiar,
touching your shoulder.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
You check the weather before you leave.
Every single time.
Not for rain,
not for wind,
just to see if it matches the forecast in your head—
The one where you’re both outside,
breathing the same air.
Some days,
the temperature feels like a lie.
It’s not cold.
It’s just missing you.
I won’t ask why you still do this.
Neither will the sky.

The porch light stayed on all summer.
You stopped leaving it off,
not out of kindness,
just…
no one asked why anymore.
The neighbors waved,
and you waved back,
but the bulb burned brighter
than any explanation.
Some doors,
you realize,
only open from the inside.
Let them wonder.

You are the hush between tides,
where the ocean forgets its own name,
just for a moment,
letting the sand decide what stays.
Some nights,
I unclench my jaw,
and the stars slip in,
cool as river stones on my tongue.
The world hums,
but you,
you are the pause,
the breath before the next wave,
where nothing needs to be saved.

You’re holding a dandelion clock in your palm.
The seeds are trembling,
not from wind,
just the weight of being alive.
Some moments refuse to stay,
like light on a closing door.
You try to count the hours,
but they dissolve before your fingers.
This is the gift,
not the loss:
You learn to let go
before the stem snaps.
Let them drift.

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
It’s not the tears. It’s the rain check you wrote yourself.
The wipers keep time,
but the road doesn’t care.
Your breath fogs the window—
a temporary galaxy
no one else will name.
The radio plays static hymns,
and you hum along,
badly.
No one hears the wrong notes.
You’re not broken.
Just a little too soft for this highway.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like an empty train station,
waiting for the last departure.
Your suitcase clicks on the platform,
and I realize
I’ve been holding my breath for weeks.
The air tastes like rain that won’t fall,
like promises dissolving into mist.
I don’t ask you to stay,
I just memorize the way your shadow
stretches long across the floor,
reaching for me one last time.
The door closes,
but the echo lingers.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
Your keys still jingle in my pocket.
I reach for them,
but the door I unlock isn’t mine.
Some mornings,
the weight of your name
bends the metal like wet paper.
I answer every call,
not because I’m brave,
but because the silence after
would be louder than the breaking.
The house stands,
even when the beams crack.
send this to them.

The old fisherman knew—
too many tides
pass beneath the same dock.
water forgets the shape of the boat
but the wood remembers the weight.

you stayed
not for the light
but the way it pooled
in the cracks you refused to patch.

some anchors
are just rust holding on.

What do you call it when freedom feels like a burden
it weighs on me
Slowly unraveling the threads
of a tapestry so intricate
The hands that once held mine
now hold a mirror to my soul
I see the beauty in the cracks
but the light shines through the brokenness
Some people find solace in the shards
And I won't let them define me
not in this moment
softly, I let go.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
I still think of your fingers
they curled around mine
slowly becoming a memory
But the feeling stays
some people don't understand
the weight of a single touch
I won't let it fade
and the ghost of it haunts me
not in a bad way
Just a reminder
of the one I think about first
in the morning.

I'm in the attic. 
some memories remain, 
Faded Polaroids 
and old trunks 
just gathering dust 
Some people call it clutter 
but The hands that crafted 
each item are still 
with me, 
in this space, 
I am free. 
and the smell of old books

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
Fragile threads.
I hold them,
not too tight
but still,
Some people weave
their own fabric,
and it's beautiful,
the intricate patterns
of a life chosen,
The hands that craft
are worn and weary,
but they keep moving,
forward, slowly.

you are more than my favorite person
i feel
at home in your presence
still i search
for a sense of belonging
AND THEN I REALIZE
it's not a place
we just
find comfort
in the folds of familiar arms
and the warmth of a gentle voice

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
The suitcase is packed for one
I'm still holding
your favorite shirt and
slowly it's losing scent
Some people leave
and take the memories
but not the smell
of your perfume on me
I won't wash it away
not yet
just a little longer.
Period

What do you call it when
the weight of others
becomes your burden
not by choice
but by habit
Some people don't notice
the load you carry
and slowly forget
the weight they've added
I won't hold it forever
not out of strength
but out of love
fading away.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
Marcus Aurelius wrote
in the ordinary
moments we find
our true character, it's
revealed in the way we
react to everyday things
like a warm cup of tea
on a chilly morning, or
the feeling of sand between
our toes, it's the little
things that make life
beautiful, isn't it
and in these moments

scripts.topic: Grieving Someone Who Did Not Die
scripts.script:
I never told you
slowly unraveling threads
Frayed edges of our tapestry
And the memories that remain
Gently folded like a letter
in a forgotten drawer
but the scent of old paper stays
SLOWLY REMINDING ME
of the way you'd weave stories
in the pause between words
and the soft creases of your fingers
tracing the lines of a map
send this to them.

How do you describe a heartbeat
that doesn’t race
but hums
steady and low
like a river carving stone
not with force
but patience
Some loves don’t crash into you
they settle
like sediment
forming land you didn’t know was missing
until your feet remember solid ground
let them know.

It doesn't feel empty. Not exactly.
they're using you
Slowly forgetting the faces
Some people need a canvas
but your colors are fading
and the brushstrokes hurt
I won't be your masterpiece
Not just a reflection
of their own desires slowly
Fading to black. Period

The cursor blinks on an empty page. 
   Slowly 
   The weight of unspoken words accumulates 
   And memories of laughter linger, 
   FINGERS HOVERING over the keyboard 
   but the courage to express falters 
   In the quiet hours, thoughts swirl 
   Around the unsent messages 
   Gathering dust in the digital space 
   Forgotten drafts, a testament to 
   The fear of vulnerability, 
   Send this to them.

There's a softness that unfolds
in secret places
SLOWLY becoming the shape
of your own presence
and the contours of your breath
FINDING solace in the folds
of a well-worn blanket
that still holds the warmth
of a thousand nights
but NEVER truly yours
UNTIL now
it's yours to keep.
 Send this to Quietlyy.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
Monday morning. The city sleeps
I'm still
choosing you
and the mess we made
Some people don't understand
but the hands that hold yours
slowly learn
not to let go
I won't make it easy
for you to leave
just be here
with me. send this to them.

I carry it everywhere
slowly unraveling
The threads that weave my story
but tangled in their minds
Some people hear my voice
and think they know the melody
but I won't let them define
the rhythm of my heart
not even in whispers
and slowly I move on
I am a tapestry
frayed edges. Period.

You're tracing the rim of your coffee cup.
Condensation beads like tiny promises.
The steam curls,
but you don’t see it.
IT’S THE SPACE BETWEEN YOUR FINGERS,
where the porcelain cools too fast.
You press harder,
and the heat remembers you.
SLOWLY, the cup hums back to life,
vibrating with the quiet of a held breath.
You exhale.
The coffee doesn’t rush to fill the silence.
Send this to them.

Sunday morning. The city sleeps
I'm still trying
and searching for a ghost
Some people don't feel
the weight of my efforts
but slowly they fade
Not out of choice
I won't give up easily
just holding on tight
and losing myself
in the emptiness. send this to them.

I still feel your absence
in every fold
of the blanket we shared
slowly losing its warmth
but memories linger
on the threads that bind
Some nights I won't sleep
and just stare
at the empty space
not searching for answers
just the ghosts of us.
let them know.

There's a hollowness that echoes deep
I'm searching
for a ghost in my reflection
but slowly losing
the will to recognize
Some people call it evolution
the hands that shaped me
are no longer mine
I won't recognize myself
in the eyes of strangers
and quietly I'm fading
away.

The bookmark was still in our place
slowly
Gentle fingers intertwined with mine
in the worn pages of a shared book
Softly turning
NEW Chapters ahead
but pages dog-eared with memories
And the words that made us laugh
still bring smiles
Quietlyy
send this to them.

There's a silence that gathers
in the folds of your absence
Slowly
it becomes a weight
that presses upon my skin
GENTLY
it reminds me of the warmth
you once brought to my mornings
and the way your fingers
interlaced with mine
But the memory of your touch
LINGERS
on my fingertips
send this to Quietlyy.

scripts.topic: The Kind of Love That Doesn't Shout
scripts.script:
What do you call it when love becomes a murmur
it stays
in the corners of your heart
slowly unfolding
like the petals of a flower
Some people call it patience
but it's more than that
it's the gentle hum of a violin
not a shout
just a constant presence
I won't let it fade.
let them know.

The first page is dog-eared for two.
slowly
Fingers tracing the same sentences
And the words that brought us to this place
Gently unfold like a worn manuscript
But the narrative is now mine to write
NEW CHAPTERS await
Slowly the characters become clearer
And the story of self-respect unfolds
Quietly the plot thickens with each passing day
And the author's voice is finally heard.
Send this to Quietlyy.

I never told you
about the weight
it's a boulder
slowly crushing me
Some people forget
the pain they cause
but I remember
the hands that hurt
I won't forgive
not out of hate
but out of scars. Period.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
Fragments of memories linger
slowly
in forgotten places
and whispered secrets
Some people keep them alive
but not in their hearts
just in the spaces
where love used to be
I won't forget the way
it felt to be whole
send this to them.

There's a longing that feels like home
I'm searching
for a place to rest
slowly unfolding like a map
Some people find it in strangers
but I find it in your eyes
not in the way you look
Just in the way you see me
I won't let go
of the feeling
that I am found. send this to them.

Distance changes everything
slowly
it seeps into daily routines
and the smell of familiar places
Some people learn to live with it
but the ache remains
just a dull hum
not loud enough to hear
but loud enough to feel
I won't forget the way
it feels to be near
send this to them.

Lao Tzu wrote
in stillness there's a strength
that permeates everything
it's the calm at the center
of the most turbulent storm, it guides us
through life's chaotic moments
when the world outside is overwhelming
i find solace in the quiet
it's where i discover my own resilience
it's where i learn to let go
and just be, softly unfolding
in the stillness, i am free

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
I never told you, quietlyy
soft fingers intertwined
in the warmth of a familiar bed
Slowly, your presence becomes
a habitual comfort, and
FINGERTIPS graze my skin
Gently, as if not to wake
the stillness between us
but my eyes, they drift
To the space where your head rests
On the pillow, a gentle impression
send this to them.

There's a hollowness that settles
in my chest
but it's where you reside
Slowly it becomes
a familiar ache
Some people can't see it
the hands that hold me
are the same hands that
I won't let go
not even in dreams
and the emptiness stays
quietly yours.

The kettle still whistles
for two
and I wait
slowly realizing
some hands are meant to hold
not force
the weight of another
but gently cradle
just as the river
NOT OUT OF FEAR
but out of respect. Softly.

scripts.topic: The Dreams Where They Come Back
scripts.script:
You're searching for a glimpse of them
in every face
but their smile is the only one
that ever fit
and slowly you're forgetting
the sound of their laughter
Some memories don't fade
they just become harder to reach
but the dreams where they come back
are always in focus
I won't wake up
quietly.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
There's a vulnerability that seeps
into my soul
but slowly turns
I won't be held
by the chains of doubt
some people find
AND THEY REACH
and grasp for my hand
not out of need
BUT OUT OF LOVE
slowly fades
in your eyes. Period

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
I carry it everywhere.
Softly unraveling threads,
and tapestries of memories
Gently woven, NOT YET COMPLETE
But the beauty lies
in the loose strands,
Slowly intertwining our lives
In a delicate, unfinished dance,
Where every step reveals
A NEW FABRIC OF US
and the gentle touch
of fingers that still hold,
send this to Quietlyy.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Who They Used to Be
scripts.script:
What do you call it when someone's flame
flickers
and warmth becomes a memory
slowly fading like a watermark
on a page that's been turned too many times
Some people hold on to the ember
but it's not the same
I won't pretend to understand
the distance between who they were
and who they've become
not out of anger
just a quiet longing. Period

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
You're standing at the edge of departure. 
Slowly. 
YOUR Suitcase Is Already Packed. 
memories of laughter 
AND Unspoken Promises 
linger on your fingertips. 
Forgotten melodies. 
SOMEONE is still humming the tune. 
Gently. 
in the hollow of your chest, 
A Fading Photograph 
of our entwined fingers. 
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What Truly Matters in the End
scripts.script:
I carry it everywhere
slowly losing grip
But hands that crafted
and the memories linger
Some people don't understand
the weight of a single thread
I won't let it unravel
not yet
but it's fraying
and I'm searching
for the right stitch
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Person You Had to Let Go to Save Yourself
scripts.script:
The bookmark was still in their novel
I turned the page
slowly forgetting
The hands that held mine
were now just a memory
but the words remain
Some people don't erase
the marks you leave
I won't rewrite
the story we had
and the ending
is just
empty pages.

scripts.topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
scripts.script:
Fragile threads.
entwining hearts, 
slowly forming 
a delicate fabric, 
but gently holding 
Some people together, 
the hands that weave 
are often unseen, 
and the beauty lies 
in the intricate patterns.

It doesn't feel empty. 
Slowly 
fingers tracing old conversations 
AND EVERY WORD 
is a delicate fossil 
but fragile 
FINGERTIPS DANCE 
Across the screen 
memories 
Slowly unravel

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
It doesn't feel like home. 
Slowly 
familiar walls become 
prisons of routine 
BUT 
Gently your fingers 
interlace with mine 
AND 
in this fleeting touch 
EXISTENCE 
becomes living 
Softly 
the world slows down 
send this to Quietlyy.

Homes are rebuilt after fires.
Slowly
but with every beam
the memory of warmth
fades into new walls
and unfamiliar smells.
Some People
hold onto the ashes
not out of sadness
but out of comfort.
Just a reminder
of what once was.
Peaceful nights.

scripts.topic: The Last Text You Never Sent
scripts.script:
There's a fatigue that clings
Slowly
to the curves of my fingers
where your words used to rest
And the thought of typing
your name in a message
feels like trying to hold water
in my palms
but it slips away
GENTLY I recall
the draft I never sent
and the words that still linger
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
There's a hollowness that settles
Slowly
in the creases of a worn cushion
where your presence used to fill
the space between us
it's a heaviness
AND IT'S MADE OF forgotten afternoons
and the soft whisper of an unmade bed
but the sheets still hold the shape of you
SOMETIMES I find myself
smoothing out the wrinkles
and the emptiness remains
send this to Quietlyy.

I carry it everywhere. 
Slowly 
forgotten photographs 
And memories we made in secret 
Become the lessons I teach myself 
Gently 
new pages turn 
But the ink still bleeds 
Through the lines of my palms 
Fragile 
as the petals of a flower 
Send this to Quietlyy.

I still remember it vividly
frozen
but the details are hazy
slowly coming back
The hands that held mine
were gentle
not like the ones I'm used to
Some people say it's a myth
that a single moment can change you
I won't forget the feeling
it's etched in my skin
send this to them.

You're holding on
 
tight
 
to the threads of a fraying rope
 
but slowly
 
the fibers start to unravel
 
Some people are anchors
 
And they hold you in place
 
not because they're strong

The hands that weave a safety net

you're still standing.

I carry it everywhere. 
slowly unfolding

TO ACCEPT THE FOLDS

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
Your fingers brush mine
softly
as we pass things
between us
in the everyday rituals
that make up our lives
we just
find comfort
in these tiny touches
Still
I NOTICE the way
you smell like fresh bread
Freshly baked
and it warms me
inside

Maya Angelou wrote
in the depths of your belly
a spark remains
it flickers with every breath,
waiting to ignite
the courage to begin again
stirs in my chest like a bird
yearning to spread its wings
and take to the open sky
where the air is crisp and clean
and the world stretches out before me
like an unfurled canvas
waiting for the brushstrokes of my soul

There's a restlessness that hums
low
in the belly of my nights
and slowly seeps into
my waking thoughts
But the hands that cradle warmth
Some people find solace
in the creases of a well-worn book
I won't forget the smell

just the feel of it remains
Softly fades.

How do you name the ache
when your shoulders remember their weight
but your arms have forgotten how to let go?
The suitcase stays packed
not because you’re leaving
just because you can’t find the zipper.
Some hands were built to hold
others to release,
and yours learned the wrong lesson first.
The floorboards creak under footsteps
that aren’t yours anymore,
but the imprint lingers like wet sand.
What if the kindest thing you ever did
was finally walk away
empty-handed.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
I never told you
about the petals

when I say I love you

and the scent of blooming

GENTLY warming my fingers

but ancient truths remain
The hands that wrote them
knew the weight of happiness
Not a feeling but a choice
just like the ink that fades
and the paper that yellows
I won't tell you it's easy
but it's yours to make
in the stillness. Period

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
the sleeves of your sweater still smell like them
Slowly
forgotten books on our shelf
And the pages that were marked
by a piece of paper with a note
in handwriting that is not mine
BUT still feels like home
New mornings CAPITALIZE
the emptiness of our space
Gently
the way I fold the laundry
is a reminder of what we used to share
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
It doesn't ache. Not anymore.
Softly,
Fragile porcelain in your hands
And the weight of what's unchangeable
Gently,
FINGERS Brushing against the cracks
Slowly forgetting
The art of mending
But WHISPERS Of a gentle hush
CALMNESS descends like a feather
In the stillness, I find solace
Send this to Quietlyy.

What do you call it when
I'm rooted
in the warmth of your presence
slowly becoming
the home I never had
Some people search for it
in crowded rooms
but I found it
in the folds of your fingers
not in the grand gestures
I won't let go
of this feeling
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
You're sitting with the weight of others
Slowly
on your shoulders, a gentle hum
of responsibility, a softness that
allows the rough edges of those
around you to find comfort
IN THE SPACE YOU CREATE
and slowly, they forget
the times you've been bruised
But gently, you offer
your palms, open and inviting
again, a canvas for their stories
send this to Quietlyy.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
How do you name the ache
when their sleeve brushes yours
but the air between you
feels like a locked door.

The coffee goes cold,
not from time,
from the way your hands
avoid the empty chair.

Some nights,
their breathing is a metronome
counting the space
where words used to live.

You trace the cracks in the ceiling,
pretending they’re constellations
that lead back to each other.

Let them know.

I stopped deleting your name from my drafts.
It lingers like a fingerprint on glass,
soft but impossible to wipe clean.
The cursor blinks,
a metronome counting every breath I didn’t take.
I type the words,
then watch them dissolve like sugar in rain.
The screen stays dark,
a secret pocket I keep just for you.
Sometimes I open it,
like a locket that doesn’t click shut.
The messages stay,
safe in their quiet,
unread but never unsent.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
There's a longing that settles in my bones
slowly
it's the smell of old books
And Forgotten memories
the hands that held mine
not to guide me
but to keep me close
Some people crave excitement
not me
I'll take the quiet mornings
with you. Period

Sunday evening. The week hasn’t started.
Your phone still hums,
but the messages aren’t for you.
Some people keep you,
like a spare key,
useful only when the door is locked.
The same hands that dial your number
slowly erase your name,
replacing it with someone else’s.
I won’t ask why you stayed,
quiet and steady,
not out of need,
but out of habit.
They’ll remember you,
when the lock breaks again.

The last text still glows on airplane mode.
It’s 3 a.m. and the sheets remember your shape.
I trace the outline,
wondering if peace is just muscle memory.
The kettle hums,
but the steam rises without asking.
I count my breaths like loose change,
each one a quiet transaction.
The mirror holds my gaze,
not unkind,
just honest.
I don’t flinch.
This stillness isn’t surrender.
It’s the first draft of belonging.

You're peeling an orange by the sink.
The rind curls like a question mark.
No one asked you to,
but you keep going.
Every strip falls,
soft as the way you used to sigh against my collarbone.
The fruit stays whole,
bright beneath the kitchen lamp.
You don't have to share this.
The juice runs down your wrist,
sticky proof you can still feed yourself.
Eat it slow.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Friends Who Only Call When They Need You
scripts.script:
You don’t need them. Not really.
A phone ringing like a vending machine,
quarters clinking in the slot.
I press my palm to the screen,
warm the glass before answering.
They ask for change,
never the time.
The call ends,
I’m still holding the silence
like a receipt I’ll never redeem.
The drawer stays closed.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
The last voicemail still plays on shuffle.
I skip it every time,
but today,
I let it run.
Your laugh,
bright as a soda can popping open,
suddenly fizzy in the quiet kitchen.
I press repeat.
The counter holds two mugs,
unwashed,
still holding yesterday’s sugar rim.
I drink from yours.
It tastes like forgetting,
but the sink remembers.
send this to them.

Hafiz whispered
beneath the laughter
there’s a room
no one enters.

you’ve learned to keep it locked
not from shame
but from fear
they might leave
if they saw the cracks.

sometimes the smile is just
a door left ajar.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
There's a longing that hums like a fridge,
constant,
invisible.
Your name on my lips
like the last sip of tea
still warm in the cup.
I reheat it,
again,
again—
The steam never stays.
Some flavors are just memories
waiting to cool.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in my ribs.
Every inhale a secret,
every exhale borrowed strength.
The teacup in my hands never empties,
but the steam has long since faded.
People praise how steady I pour,
how the sugar dissolves without stirring.
No one tastes the salt I hide in the sugar bowl.
My smile is a well-rehearsed recipe,
perfectly sweet,
perfectly still,
perfectly hollow.
Send this to them.

YOU HOLD the weight
like a held breath
unseen
steady
while the world spins faster
and the clock hands beg for mercy
YOU
stay
rooted in the hush
the kind that hums
underneath the rush
where muscles remember
what bones forget
and the quiet
isn’t empty
just full of you
waiting.
visual_keywords: a single sock left on the floor

scripts.script:
The porch swing still creaks at midnight.
I counted every sway,
like a pulse
I couldn’t slow.
The clock hands moved,
but time didn’t.
Some nights,
I swore the chains
remembered your weight.
Not the leaving—
the way you’d lean back,
trusting gravity
to hold us both.
I stopped waiting
when the creaking stopped.

There's a warmth that doesn't burn,
like a kettle left to hum,
just waiting,
not for tea,
but for the sound of your key,
the door that never locks,
not out of trust,
but because you already know,
the way back is lit,
by the scent of old books,
and the creak of the floor,
that only answers to you,
let them know.

It's 4:17. The kettle hums.
Your coffee cup still warm,
the one with the chip on the rim.
I trace the crack,
like the way you say my name—
not loud,
just there,
in the space between thoughts.
Some mornings,
I pretend not to notice.
But the sugar bowl is full,
the spoon already stirred,
and I know it's you,
quietly,
keeping the world from falling apart.

Falling out of love is a slow leak,
not a storm.
The air escapes
one breath at a time,
and you don’t notice
until the room feels hollow.
The same couch,
the same coffee mug,
but the weight of your name
on my tongue
is lighter now.
I won’t call it betrayal,
just gravity.
We’re drifting,
not apart,
but into different orbits.
The door stays open,
but the draft is colder.
I’ll miss the shape of you
less each day.

scripts.topic: Why Peace Is Not the Absence of Storm
scripts.script:
Peace isn’t the sky holding its breath
waiting for thunder to leave
it’s the way your fingers
still
trace circles on my back
after the sirens fade.
I KNOW THIS
because the roof leaks
and you laugh
while we move the bed
toward the drip,
toward the sound
of rain we can’t control.

scripts.topic: What I'd Tell You If I Could
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the hours
we spent tangled in the same sheet.
The way your breath curled
like smoke from a forgotten cigarette,
softly dissolving into morning.
I never told you
I kept the receipt from that diner,
the one where you stole my fries.
It’s creased in my wallet,
still smelling like grease and your laughter.
And if I could,
I’d send this to you
before the coffee gets cold.

Some doors don’t creak when they close.
They just stop inviting you in,
and you realize
no one ever locked them,
you just expected them to open.
The hinge never needed oil,
just time to rust in peace.
Not every path is meant to be walked,
some are only meant to be seen
from the window of a moving train.
You don’t have to force the view,
just let the scenery change.

scripts.topic: The Part You Never Talk About
scripts.script:
THE PART YOU NEVER TALK ABOUT
isn’t the fall
it’s the way
you caught yourself
mid-air
before anyone saw
how close you came
to shattering
YOU SMOOTHED YOUR HAIR
you laughed
like it was nothing
and the room
kept spinning
visual_keywords: a half-empty coffee cup left on a radiator
fingers tracing the rim of a glass

scripts.topic: The Last Text You Never Sent
scripts.script:
It’s not regret. Just a pocket,
empty now,
where the words used to jingle.
The screen glows,
soft as a held breath,
but the thumb hesitates—
not afraid,
just knowing
some doors only open inward.
You don’t delete it.
You don’t send it.
You let the battery die.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
The fisherman murmurs
across the tide
some drift
some cast
some let the current decide.

you exist when the net is empty
but you live
when the sea remembers your name.

the gulls don’t ask why
only where the next wave breaks.
listen,
not for answers
but the salt on your skin

Fragile threads unravel
we just
let go
slowly forgetting
the way it used to be
and somewhere in the haze
of FORGOTTEN MEMORIES
we find solace
still searching
for a glimpse
of what could've been
fading fast

I stopped waiting for a seat at the table.
The chairs were mine all along,
just covered in dust and someone else’s rules.
I pulled one out,
slowly brushed away the ghosts of expectations,
and sat down.
Not to be seen,
but to taste the meal I cooked.
Some nights the room feels empty,
but the silence is mine,
and the fork fits my hand perfectly.
I belong here.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like fresh laundry
on the line at dusk.
Your laugh lands
soft as the first shirt
I pull down still warm.
The way you stir honey
into tea without counting drops.
How your socks never match
yet your steps never falter.
I memorize the shape of your yawn
like a secret alphabet.
Your thumbprint on my coffee cup
is the only constellation I need.
send this to them.

Some nights I trace the edges,
of a love written in pencil.
Not ink,
not carved,
just soft graphite lines,
that smudge if I press too hard.
I keep the paper folded,
in my back pocket,
where it warms against my skin.
No one sees,
but the creases remember,
every time I unfold,
every time I let it breathe.

Hafiz whispered
like a child chasing fireflies
you think you're catching them
but really
they're teaching you to glow.

we clutch maps
when the path is made of footsteps
not ink.
your hands tremble
not from fear
but from holding too many tomorrows.

let the road hum
beneath your soles—
it knows the way.

scripts.topic: What Truly Matters in the End
scripts.script:
The last page of the atlas,
still warm from the printer’s press,
folded into a paper airplane,
thrown from a moving train,
just to see where it lands.
Somewhere between here,
and the edge of the map,
the ink bleeds into rivers,
and the rivers remember your name.
Not the one you were given,
but the one you carried,
like a pocketful of seeds,
planted in the cracks of sidewalks,
where no one else thought to look.

You don’t fear the fall.
Just the landing.
The way your ribs remember impact,
but your knees forget how to bend.
Somewhere between the last step
and the first,
you become a question no one asks.
The pavement doesn’t care if you’re ready—
only if you’ll crack.
And you do.
Not like glass,
like a door left open in winter.
Let them see the draft.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
The spoon still rests on your napkin.
Morning chooses you first,
warm syrup pooling where your thumbprints dried.
I watch the way the kitchen hums,
only when you’re in it.
BUT the toaster pops,
and suddenly I’m not hungry for anything else.
YOU leave crumbs on the counter,
like tiny breadcrumbs back to me.
The clock ticks,
but time feels softer,
like dough you’ve already kneaded.
send this to them.

I stopped waiting for the tide to turn.
The shore remembers every footprint,
but the ocean doesn’t apologize.
I unclenched my fists,
let the saltwater pool in my palms.
It stings,
then it doesn’t.
The horizon doesn’t ask me to swim,
only to float.
I drift,
and the current holds me anyway.
Send this to them.

You’re folding the last shirt.
A sleeve still holds your shape.
The drawer closes softer than you expected.
No slam,
no finality—
just the quiet click of wood meeting wood.
You leave the hanger spinning,
slow figure-eight in empty air.
The room breathes,
unburdened.
Your scent lingers like a half-erased chalk name.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
You trace the rim of your coffee cup.
Cold porcelain remembers your lips better than I do.
I watch the steam curl,
and wonder if you notice the way my hands shake.
Not from nerves.
From the quiet ache of wanting to press them against your back.
The sugar in your tea dissolves,
but I’m still here,
sweetening the air between us.
You laugh at something on your screen.
I memorize the sound,
store it like loose change in my pockets.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling.
Every night you traced them with your finger,
blindfolded,
like a cartographer mapping my broken sky.
The plaster didn’t heal,
but your touch turned the fractures into constellations.
I never asked for the stars,
just the steady weight of your hand
pressing warmth into the hollows.
You didn’t fix me,
you stayed,
and the cracks stopped feeling like wounds.
send this to them.

I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling.
Every night they branch like rivers,
flowing toward the same ocean.
Happiness isn’t the shore,
It’s the current pulling me under.
I trace the map with my fingertips,
but the ink never dries.
Somewhere between drowning and floating,
I learned to breathe water.
The waves don’t ask for names.
They just carry what’s already there.
send this to them.

5:17 pm. The kettle hums one note.
Your fingers trace the rim of my cup,
not drinking,
just circling,
like you’re memorizing the shape of my thirst.
I won’t ask what you’re thinking—
some questions are bridges
and you’re already halfway across.
The steam writes your name in the air,
then dissolves.
I knew then
I’d spend years
trying to read what the air erased.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
There's a joy that hums beneath the skin,
like a pocket full of river stones.
Each one smooth,
each one chosen.
We polish them in our palms,
not for their weight,
but for the way they catch the quiet.
Some days,
the meaning isn't found—
it's carried.
Like a lantern,
low and steady,
through rooms we've already memorized.
send this to them.

The house exhaled,
its ribs unclenched.
Every hinge,
every floorboard,
remembered how to breathe without us.
Some rooms,
like old photographs,
kept their edges soft.
The clock’s hands,
now free of duty,
danced in slow circles.
And the air,
suddenly thick with absence,
settled like dust on a forgotten shelf.

I stopped counting the hours
since your last text arrived like a paper crane
folded just for me.
The creases still hold your fingerprint,
soft as the way you said my name once,
only once,
and I replayed it until the syllables dissolved.
No one else knows how the air changes
when you walk into a room,
but I do—
it tastes like rain on hot pavement.
I keep this secret in my throat,
where it hums without sound.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the steps between us.
Your sleeve brushes mine,
and the whole room tilts.
I memorize the way your coffee cup rests,
half-empty,
on the nightstand we built from mismatched books.
No one notices how the air hums,
just a little,
when you exhale near my collarbone.
I could stay here,
watching your fingers trace circles on the table,
until the last train leaves without us.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Being the One Who Always Checks In
scripts.script:
You scroll. Pause. Scroll again.
The screen glows like a campfire,
warm but never yours to keep.
Some nights,
you’re the one who remembers birthdays,
who asks about their day,
who holds the conversation when it stutters.
Not out of pity,
but because you know
the weight of empty inboxes.
The last message sent at 3am
stays blue,
unopened.
You still check in anyway.

scripts.topic: What I'd Tell You If I Could
scripts.script:
I’d leave the porch light on
for you
if I thought you’d come
back for it
but the bulb’s been dead
three summers now
and still
you never ask
WHY I HAVEN’T FIXED IT
so I guess
you already know
I stopped waiting
before you stopped trying

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that only aches,
not for food,
but for the shape of your name
pressed into my palm.
The scar remembers,
even when I forget,
that pain was the first language
you ever taught me.
Some gifts arrive wrapped in gauze,
and we spend years
peeling back the layers,
only to find the wound
was the map all along.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
You don’t glow. Not like that.
Your edges hum,
soft static where skin meets cotton.
I count the freckles you hide,
three above your collarbone,
two near your wrist—
constellations you never name.
The way you stir your tea,
clockwise,
three slow circles,
then pause.
I memorize the shape of your hesitation.
Send this to them.

SOFTLY we awaken
to our own value
we just
started saying no
started setting boundaries
and somewhere in the mess
of learning to prioritize ourselves
WE FIND OUR VOICE
still whispering
GENTLY to our souls
that we are enough
in this quiet revolution
of self-respect

I stopped waiting for your text to glow.
The screen stayed black,
like a tide pool after the moon leaves.
My ribs no longer hummed the old tune,
just the quiet creak of a porch swing
no one sits on anymore.
I traced the salt lines on my wrist,
realized they weren’t tears
but the map of somewhere new.
The ocean still arrives,
but now it brings different shells.
I keep the smoothest one in my pocket.
send this to them.

A single thread pulled loose,
unraveling the hem of your sleeve.
I caught it,
not to mend,
but to follow where it led—
across streets that remembered your name,
past cafés humming old tunes,
to the corner where the rain
hadn’t washed your footsteps away.
Some nights,
the thread still tugs,
not asking,
just reminding me you’re there.

The river doesn’t ask for a name,
just carries the stones,
polishing edges
until they forget their shape.
Somewhere between the current and the bank,
the water learns to listen,
not to the roar,
but to the quiet hum
of what’s being smoothed away.
It doesn’t fight the weight,
just lets the weight teach it
how to flow.

I still taste the salt
on your fingers after the beach.
The way you licked them clean,
slowly,
like a secret only we knew.
I COUNT the freckles on your shoulder
when you sleep,
but the number keeps changing.
Some days it’s seven,
some days it’s twelve—
like the universe is adding more
just to keep me here.
Your laugh is the only sound
that doesn’t need a reason.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
There’s a quiet ache that lingers
like the last page of a book
you never finish,
not from sadness,
but from knowing
some stories aren’t meant to close.
The spine stays cracked,
the words half-read,
and that’s the beauty—
not the ending,
but the wanting.
Let it stay open.

I stopped explaining the shape of my bones,
not because they’re wrong,
but because no one holds the ruler right.
Some nights,
the mattress sighs with me,
its springs counting every misread word.
I carry it in my pockets—
not stones,
just folded notes
no one ever opens.
The ink bleeds,
but the meaning stays.
Send this to them.

YOU ONCE CARRIED
my grocery bags
up five flights of stairs
without asking
and I let you
because your hands were steady
and I was tired
NOW THEY SLUMP
against my ribs
like overripe fruit
I should have set down
months ago
but still
I clutch.

scripts.topic: Why Peace Is Not the Absence of Storm
scripts.script:
How do you explain to the tide
why the shore still waits
when the waves keep leaving?
Peace is not the stillness,
just the trust that water
will always return
carrying salt and shells
and the memory of your name.
The ocean doesn’t apologize
for its storms,
only promises the next low hum.
You learn to breathe between crashes.
send this to them.

Lao Tzu whispered
before the tide turns
there is a pause
where the ocean holds its breath
and the sand forgets its name.

I think of you then
not as the wave that comes
but the stillness before it
not the footstep but the space between
where everything is possible
and nothing needs to be said.

You are my calm
before the world remembers to move.

There's a quiet like unopened mail.
Letters stacked,
soft corners curling,
no return address.
I trace your name with my thumb,
but the ink doesn’t bleed.
Some promises are just paper,
folded too many times.
I leave them on the table,
next to the half-drunk tea.
The steam forgets to rise.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
The first sip of morning,
before the cup is even warm.
I reach,
not for the kettle,
but for the shape of you in my ribs.
Some nights,
the bedsheet’s crease
becomes a shoreline,
and I trace it
with my fingertips,
pretending tides still pull me toward you.

There's a hunger that doesn't ask for food,
just pieces of you,
slowly carved out,
until the plate is empty.
The hands that fed you,
now hold the knife,
and you smile,
not from joy,
but from habit.
Some loves are mirrors,
reflecting only what they need,
until you forget your own face.
I won't erase,
to keep them whole.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
I kept the thermostat at your number
not
because you liked it
but
because I could still pretend
YOUR HAND
would reach for mine
in the cold.
The fridge hums
old songs we never danced to
and I finally
unplugged the clock
that only ticked
when you texted.

Sunday evening. The kettle hums low.
I trace your name
on the steamy window,
not to remember,
just to feel the shape of it.
Some nights,
you’re the only story my hands know by heart.
The pages don’t need words—
just the weight of your voice
pressed between them.
I won’t call you perfect,
just the one who fits
when the world doesn’t.
let them know.

YOU SWALLOWED THE STORM
in one small curve
like a spoonful of sugar
that never melts
just sits
heavy on the tongue
YOU PERFECTED THE ART
of looking happy
while the house burned down
behind your teeth
no one noticed
the smoke
until you stopped smiling

You're stirring the tea too hard.
The leaves swirl,
but the cup stays cold.
Some truths don't steep,
they dissolve
only when you stop forcing the flavor.
The kettle hums,
and you realize
it was never about the heat,
just the waiting.
Let it sit.
The warmth finds you.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
Hafiz knew
love isn’t a cure
it’s a companion
who sits with you
through the cracks.
You didn’t fix me—
you held the lantern
while I stumbled
past the broken tiles,
not to mend them
but to see
where they led.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Who They Used to Be
scripts.script:
The voicemail greeting was never changed.
I press play,
just to hear the old lilt,
the one that used to skip like stones.
Now it’s a museum,
quiet glass between us.
Your new laugh,
bright and unfamiliar,
lands like a coin in a fountain.
I keep tossing mine in,
hoping they’ll touch.
The water never gives them back.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that doesn't ask for food.
It gnaws at the edges of your ribs
like a houseplant learning to speak.
You water it,
but the soil stays dry.
EXISTING is a receipt you never signed for.
Living is the moment you crumple it,
watch the ink bleed into something new.
The air changes when you decide to stay.
Not because you have to,
but because the walls remember your name.
Send this to them.

Hafiz knew
love was never the whole story.
even the deepest well
dries in drought.
what you mistook for thirst
was only the first sip.

you gave everything
the vase cracked
the flowers wilted
you kept watering the roots.

the garden remembers
your hands.

Your name fits
in my palm
like a pebble
I found by the river
and kept
for no reason
just to roll between fingers
when the day feels too wide
YOUR VOICE
soft as the hum
of a fridge in another room
I lean into
without thinking
and then
it’s gone

scripts.topic: What Truly Matters in the End
scripts.script:
How do you measure a life
that was never meant to be counted?
The clock hands move,
but the heart keeps its own time.
Some moments stretch like taffy,
slow and sweet,
while others snap in an instant.
The hands that held you,
the ones that let go,
they all leave fingerprints on your ribs.
Not the kind that bruise,
just the kind that remind you
what truly matters in the end.

You’re tracing circles on my wrist,
just below the pulse.
The way your finger hesitates—
like a bee deciding which petal to trust.
I didn’t know bodies could memorize,
but mine learned the shape of your pause.
Every second stretched,
thin as the skin over my veins.
You looked up,
and the room tilted.
That’s how I knew.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I Mean When I Say I Love You
scripts.script:
The salt shaker is still half-full.
We never finished it.
Your laugh lingers like the last drop,
clinging,
refusing to fall.
I trace the rim with my thumb,
remembering the shape of your fingers.
No grand declarations,
just the quiet hum of knowing.
The way you butter toast exactly how I like,
without asking.
This,
this is what I mean.
send this to them.

How do you explain the way
roads remember your footsteps
long after you’ve turned away?
The pavement hums,
just a quiet vibration under soles,
not asking,
only holding the shape of your stride.
Some journeys don’t need maps,
they unfold like a letter
written in invisible ink.
You won’t see the words
until the heat of time reveals them.
Trust the crease,
let it guide you home.

Hafiz whispered
some goodbyes are rivers
that never reach the sea.
they pool in the chest,
waiting for the dam to crack.

you carry the farewell
like a stone in your pocket—
smooth, heavy, unspoken.
no ceremony,
no final glance,
just the weight
and the quiet ache
of what could have been.
visual_keywords: pocket with a single smooth stone

scripts.script:
6:47pm. The kettle hums,
but the tea tastes like absence.
Your favorite mug,
still warm from yesterday’s touch,
slowly cools on the counter.
I trace the rim,
where your lips used to rest.
Somewhere,
you’re laughing at a joke I’ll never hear,
and the distance feels like static,
not space.
I won’t text you,
not to interrupt,
just to remember
how your voice fits here.

I still keep your number saved.
Like a ticket stub from a train,
I never boarded.
The digits glow,
soft blue on my screen,
but the destination blurs.
I press call sometimes,
just to hear the ring,
then hang up before you answer.
The voicemail tone hums,
a lullaby of what could be.
I delete the history,
but the contact stays.
send this to them.

There's a hunger that doesn't ask for food,
just the shape of your name in my mouth.
I swallow it whole,
but it lingers,
sweet and unbearable.
The way you stir your coffee,
three slow circles,
never looking up,
and I know—
this is the quietest kind of devotion.
Not grand gestures,
just the weight of your hand
on my knee,
telling me,
without words,
I'm someone worth staying for.

You're tracing circles on the fogged-up window.
Not erasing,
just following the path of your breath.
Somewhere between the glass and the sky,
you stop fighting the shape of the air.
The circle stays,
imperfect,
and that's the first time you exhale without permission.
No one taught you how to let go,
but your fingers remember the weight of nothing.
The window clears just enough
to show your reflection,
unapologetic.

YOU WERE LOOKING
for a street name
that didn’t exist yet
I was the corner
where the map folded wrong
and suddenly
we fit
like two loose threads
pulled from different sweaters
STILL TANGLED
in the lint trap
of a laundromat radio
playing our song
on a Tuesday

There's a kind of ache
like a sweater stretched too wide.
The sleeves still remember your arms,
but the fabric sags,
empty in the elbows.
I wore you like second skin,
now the seams itch with absence.
Every hanger in the closet
whispers your name,
but the door stays shut.
Some shapes only fit once.
I fold the sleeves inward,
send this to them.

The voicemail greeting hasn’t changed.
Still that laugh,
warm as a held breath.
I press call,
but the line hums empty.
Some friendships aren’t endings—
they’re echoes you mistake for answers.
The screen glows,
bright with a name that no longer rings.
I trace the letters,
slowly,
like a map to nowhere.
Let them go.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
You don’t pick me. Not like fruit.
Every morning the same drawer slides open,
quiet as a secret kept for years.
The spoon you choose is always mine—
tarnished silver,
worn smooth by my name.
No ceremony,
just the slow settling of habit.
I fit in your palm
like a coin you’ve carried so long
you forget it’s currency.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
You don’t believe in fate.
Not like this.
The clock on the wall
still ticks,
but the hands move backward sometimes.
I’ve seen them.
Not magic,
just your mind
rewriting the seconds
into something softer.
The cracks in the ceiling
aren’t flaws—they’re constellations.
You named them after hope.

There's a quiet that hums,
like a fridge in an empty kitchen.
I leave the door open,
just to hear something,
but the light doesn't help.
Some days,
the hum is all I am.
Not sadness,
not anger,
just the sound of keeping things cold.
You ask how I'm doing,
I say fine,
and the hum gets louder.
I close the door slowly.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
The porch swing still rocks,
even when I’m not there.
I leave it moving,
just for you.
Some mornings,
I swear I hear the chains creak,
soft and familiar,
like your voice on the phone.
The house doesn’t ask where you went,
but the swing does,
with every slow sway.
I answer in silence,
let it keep going.
You’re the one I think about first,
and last.

The teacup was still warm.
You held it,
not for the taste,
but the habit of holding.
Happiness isn’t the steam rising—
it’s the quiet between sips,
the way your fingers remember
even when your mind forgets.
Some truths are too old to speak,
too soft to shout.
They live in the porcelain,
in the way it never cracks,
just waits for the next pour.

The clock hands froze,
mid-tick,
like a held breath.
Somewhere,
a child stopped spinning,
top wobbling,
but not falling.
Not yet.
The room kept its shape,
walls steady,
while the air
thickened into syrup.
No more,
no less,
just still.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in my ribs
three fractures ago
but the weight still bows my spine
like a bridge carrying too many cars
Some days I forget how to breathe
without holding my breath for everyone else
The house stays warm
and the meals stay hot
and the tears stay locked behind a smile
I won’t let them hear the hinges creak
just the quiet click of the door closing
soft enough to hide the breaking.
visual_keywords: hands gripping a mug too tightly
hallway with one light left on

scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling.
Every night they branch like rivers,
carving deltas only I can name.
The mattress sags,
but so do my shoulders when I exhale.
This is how maps are made—
not with borders,
but with the weight of what you choose to carry.
I trace the seams of my own ribs,
find cities I’ve been too afraid to visit.
The quiet here isn’t empty.
It’s a room I’m finally learning to live in.
send this to them.

The way you leave the butter out.
softens everything,
not just toast.
I watch it melt
across the counter,
slowly filling the cracks
I never told you about.
YOUR HANDS still know
the exact pressure
to press the knife down.
And I,
I let the silence
lick the crumbs off my fingers.
send this to them.

I forgot how loud
rain sounds
on a tin roof
until you left
and the house
learned to hold its breath.
EVERY CREAK
became a question
no one answered.
The clock’s hands
moved like fingers
through wet sand,
leaving no trace.

They call it a language barrier,
but it’s not the words.
Somewhere between the tongue,
the throat forgets how to shape sound,
and the air comes out,
not wrong,
just untranslatable.
The hands that reach,
only brush the edges,
like trying to hold fog,
knowing it’ll slip,
knowing they’ll call it a ghost.

scripts.topic: When Love Feels Like Coming Home
scripts.script:
You're unlocking the door again.
The key turns without sound,
like it remembers your touch.
Not a creak,
not even the floorboards
whispering your name.
Some rooms
only feel this warm
