you just held the cup with me.
The tea went cold,
but the warmth stayed.
Some things don’t need mending,
they just need someone to stay.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
IT WHISPERS
like a radio left on
in another room
static humming
your name
until it doesn’t
until you forget
what you were listening for
YOU TURN
but the dial’s stuck
on the same old station
playing
all the ways you’ll never be
enough

Hafiz whispered
acceptance isn’t surrender
it’s the sky
unfolding its arms
for the storm.

you think you’re holding the reins
hands raw from gripping
but the horse knows the way
your legs only cling
to what’s already moving.

let go
and the road carries you.

scripts.topic: Loving Someone Who Can't Love Back
scripts.script:
The voicemail still saves their last goodbye.
I press play,
but the message is just static now.
Some frequencies fade faster than others.
Your name on my screen,
a ghost in the call log.
I still swipe right,
like habit is a kind of hope.
The phone hums,
empty,
like a seashell that forgot the ocean.
I keep it anyway.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
Some nights I rehearse conversations,
letters I’ll never mail,
ink bleeding into the page like a confession.
I practice the tilt of my head,
the way my voice would soften,
if I ever let the words escape.
The clock hums,
a steady metronome for my silence,
counting beats I refuse to sing.
I trace the cracks in the ceiling,
map constellations of what could be,
if only I dared to speak.
But the room stays quiet,
and the stars keep their secrets.

The sugar bowl was half-empty.
You didn’t ask how I take it.
I stirred anyway,
watching crystals dissolve like old doubts.
The spoon clicked against porcelain,
a metronome for the quiet between us.
I forgot to drink,
but the cup stayed warm.
You reached across,
not to hold,
just to nudge my sleeve.
I knew then.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: What Truly Matters in the End
scripts.script:
The clock’s hands dissolve like salt,
vanishing into the tide’s slow hum.
Some things don’t need to last,
just matter in the moment,
like the weight of a promise
held between two open palms.
Not the words,
but the pause where nothing’s said,
where the air still hums with trust.
The rest?
Just sand slipping through fingers,
quietly,
without regret.

The clock’s hands stitch,
backward through every word,
I never meant to say.
Some nights,
the thread snaps,
and I’m left holding loose ends,
counting each tug on my ribs.
The bed’s a stage,
rehearsing every exit,
while the audience sleeps.
I rewrite the script,
but the ink’s already dry,
just smudges where I tried.

You don’t need the ocean.
Just the tide in your chest,
quiet and predictable,
pulling you back
to the shore you built yourself.
The waves don’t ask questions,
they only return what you give.
Some nights,
you float,
not because you’re weightless,
but because the water remembers your shape.
Let it hold you.

scripts.topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
scripts.script:
I stopped checking the rearview mirror.
The road stretched wider than my hands could hold.
Your laughter used to fit in my cupped palms,
now it slips through like loose sand.
I trace the cracks in the dashboard,
counting the miles we don’t share anymore.
The radio plays songs you’d hate,
and I hum them anyway.
Some doors stay open out of habit,
not because anyone’s coming home.
I leave the porch light on,
but the fireflies have moved on.
send this to them.

You’re not the ocean.
Not even close.
Just a tide pool,
still,
but holding every color the sky refused to keep.
I press my palm to the surface,
and the ripples remember your name.
Not in waves,
in whispers,
the kind that live under skin,
not asking,
just existing.
You are my calm.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
Forgiveness is a well that never runs dry.
The bucket comes back full,
even after you’ve poured it all out.
Some hearts,
just keep giving,
not because they’re endless,
but because they remember what it feels like to thirst.
The same lips that say ‘I’m sorry’,
learn to say ‘I’ll wait’,
until waiting becomes the shape of love.
And one day,
you realize,
the well is empty,
but the thirst is gone.

You don’t push the river.
It just knows how to bend.
The current hums secrets,
soft as the underside of a leaf.
Some things learn to float
before they’re ready.
You cup your palms,
but the water slips through,
not because it’s lost—
because it’s listening.
The shore remembers your name.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the freckles on your collarbone.
Each one a tiny map I memorized,
but the way you look at me erases all coordinates.
Your gaze is a riverbed,
dry for years,
until my name falls like rain.
I never knew thirst could sound so soft.
The room tilts,
just slightly,
like a boat untethered.
I float.
Send this to them.

The safest place I know
isn’t a place at all,
just a pocket of air
between two heartbeats,
where the world tilts
and no one notices.
Some nights,
it’s the hum of a fridge
in an empty kitchen,
steady as a second moon.
I curl there,
small and unshaken,
while the rest of me orbits.

The river doesn’t apologize,
for carving paths through stone,
just moves,
leaving behind what once was whole.
Some things aren’t meant to last,
not out of cruelty,
but the quiet math of time,
adding up to change.
The hands that held you,
now hold only memory,
soft as the tide’s retreat,
leaving shells where feet once stood.

scripts.topic: Being the One Who Always Checks In
scripts.script:
There's a tenderness that hums,
like a fridge left open in the night.
You don’t notice it at first,
just the way the milk stays cold,
the door ajar,
the light spilling onto linoleum.
And you,
checking the seal,
adjusting the hinge,
closing what was never yours to keep open.
The house doesn’t thank you,
but the milk doesn’t spoil.
send this to them.

The houseplant on your desk leans left.
It’s reaching for the space you left,
but the window stays shut.
YOU TAUGHT IT how to bend,
now it won’t straighten.
A single leaf brushes the wallpaper,
like a finger tracing a name.
The soil remembers your touch,
and the water you forgot to give.
IT’S NOT dying,
just learning to belong to the air.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
The river doesn’t ask the banks to move.
It carves,
just flows where gravity pulls,
and the shore
learns to hold the shape of water.
Some days,
you are the current,
others,
the quiet stone that stays.
Not surrender,
just knowing
what bends,
and what remains.
Let the tide decide.

I FORGOT HOW TO HOLD IT
like sand
slipping
between my fingers
not a storm
just the tide
pulling back
I WATCHED THE WAVES
carry it away
not angry
not sad
just gone
and the shore
breathed easier

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
Your sweater still hangs on my chair.
It smells like the bakery we passed,
that morning it rained just enough
to make the pavement steam.
I trace the stitches with my thumb,
pretending they’re the freckles I memorized
while you weren’t looking.
The clock ticks,
but the hands don’t move.
I write your name in the fog on my mirror,
then wipe it away before it dries.
This ache is a slow-burning wick,
and I keep forgetting to blow it out.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Healing Hurts More Than the Wound
scripts.script:
The stitches ache worse than the cut.
Not the blood,
just the pull,
tightening,
thread through tender skin.
Nobody warns you
about the itch of mending,
or how the body remembers pain
long after the wound forgets.
Some scars
refuse to fade,
not from damage,
but from the hands that held them closed.

Happiness is not a destination.
It lingers in the steam of morning tea,
just before the cup touches your lips,
when the world is still half-asleep
and the quiet hum of the kettle
feels like a secret only you know.
Somewhere between the first sip
and the last,
you forget to chase it,
and that’s when it finds you.
Not as a prize,
but as the breath before the day begins.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
Living is a riverbed that remembers rain,
carving curves,
while existing is the dust,
settling on the same old stone.
Some people build rafts,
patchwork dreams,
and let the current take them,
others just stand in the shallows,
counting pebbles,
never feeling the pull,
never daring to float.

scripts.topic: The Quiet Kind of Love Nobody Talks About
scripts.script:
You leave the porch light on
when I forget
to tell you I’m coming.

I bring your coffee
just how you like it
before you ask.

WE DON’T SAY MUCH
but the silence
isn’t empty—
just full of
all the things
we already know.

And when you reach for my hand
it’s not a question
it’s a quiet answer.

How do you explain the weight
in your pocket
when the phone feels like a stone?
Not heavy,
just... persistent.
The words you type at midnight
curl into question marks,
unsent,
like smoke from a cigarette you never lit.
Some nights,
the cursor blinks back tears.
You delete them anyway,
but the ache stays,
folded small,
in the drafts you’ll never open.

I leave my porch light yellow
not for you
but because
some nights
I pretend
you’re still coming
to borrow sugar
to ask about the roses
to linger just long enough
to let the screen door sigh
like it used to
when we were
almost something.

The road never asked for directions
it just
unfolded
one hesitant mile at a time
and suddenly
YOU STOPPED CHECKING THE MAP
trusting the cracks in the pavement
to lead you somewhere
worth the gas
worth the doubt
worth the way your hands
still tremble
on the wheel.

scripts.topic: The Family Member Who Hurt You Most
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the cracks in the ceiling,
tiny fault lines you never noticed before.
The quilt smells like mothballs and old apologies,
but the stitches hold,
even when the hands that made them didn’t.
You taught me how to sew,
threading needles through my skin without drawing blood.
A family of thimbles in the drawer,
none of them fit you anymore.
I still wear the one you gave me,
its metal warm against my finger,
like a secret I can’t put down.
Send this to them.

I left the keys on the counter
like a question
no one would answer.
The door didn’t slam
it just
closed.
YOU KEPT THE COUCH
I kept the habit
of waiting
for the phone to ring
then unlearned it
one dial tone at a time.
Now the hallway stretches
longer than memory
and I walk it
anyway.

There's a tenderness that cracks porcelain.
Your laughter a glaze I never learned to fire.
I hold my breath,
count the seconds between your inhales,
pretend my ribs aren't kiln-hot.
The way you trace my palm
leaves fingerprints in the dust,
soft as the first brush of glaze.
I smile,
but my edges stay raw.
Some pieces only you can mend.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
You're stirring honey into tea again.
The spoon clinks like a metronome,
steadying the room.
I watch your wrist,
so sure,
even when the world isn’t.
That slow swirl,
thick and golden,
becomes the only proof I need.
Some days,
that’s enough.
Send this to them.

I stopped counting the mornings
we woke tangled in the same sheet music.
Your breath,
a metronome I could never outrun.
The way you hum off-key in the shower,
somehow keeps the time of my ribs.
I trace the scar on your knee,
a constellation I’ve memorized in the dark.
You are the quiet between my thoughts,
the pause that makes the song worth singing.
I never told you,
but your hands are the only map I trust.
Send this to them.

You’re staring at the ceiling fan again.
Spinning,
but it’s not moving fast enough
to blur the edges of what you almost said.
The clock’s hands,
stuck in slow motion,
like they’re afraid to wake you.
You trace the cracks in the plaster,
counting every fault line
like they’re reasons to stay.
Some nights,
the quiet isn’t empty—
it’s full of everything you didn’t do.
You exhale,
and the fan stops for a second.
Then you let go.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
Your laugh lands like
sugar on my tongue
I didn’t know
it was missing
until you gave it back.
The way you stir your coffee—
three slow circles,
drag of spoon on porcelain—
makes the kitchen glow
without reason.
I watch your hands
and suddenly
I believe in magic again.

4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
but the water never boils.
You stir honey,
slowly,
like you’re afraid to wake the taste.
The spoon scrapes,
not against porcelain,
but the shape you used to be.
Some mornings,
you catch your reflection
in the microwave door,
and the face looking back
isn’t yours anymore.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: What I Never Said Out Loud
scripts.script:
How do you name the space
between apology and pride?
I kept my voice
in a jar of honey,
slowly crystallizing,
not to preserve,
but to hide the sting.
The words I never gave you
are still warm,
still golden,
still waiting to melt on your tongue.
I won’t force them down.
let them dissolve unseen.

4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
just below a whistle.
I trace the cracks in the mug,
slowly,
like reading braille for a language I’ve forgotten.
The steam rises,
not in clouds,
but in questions that dissolve before I answer.
Some mornings,
the quiet isn’t empty—
it’s a room full of ghosts
who refuse to speak.
I pour the water,
and the tea leaves bloom,
patient,
like they’ve been waiting for me.

scripts.topic: The Exhaustion of Pretending to Be Okay
scripts.script:
Hafiz knew
we wear the weight
like a borrowed coat—
too heavy for the season
but we button it tight.

you think no one sees
the stitches straining,
the seams splitting at the seams.

take it off.
the air is kinder
than your fear.
visual_keywords: empty rocking chair on a porch

scripts.script:
How do you explain to someone
that home isn’t a place at all
but a quiet hum in your chest
when their hands brush yours
and the room stops needing names?
Some doors don’t have locks
just a way of knowing
when to leave them open,
when to step inside
without knocking.
It’s the way their laugh
still fits in your pockets.
Send this to them.

The clock’s second hand,
counting cracks in the ceiling,
like a cartographer mapping regret.
Every tick,
a new fault line splits open,
and I’m just the geologist
who keeps revisiting the same earthquake.
Some nights,
the bed feels like a crime scene,
I’m the detective,
the witness,
the weapon,
all rolled into one,
still dusting for prints.

scripts.topic: The Text You Read But Did Not Reply To
scripts.script:
How do you measure the weight
of words you swallowed whole?
The cursor blinks,
steady as a metronome,
but your name stays lodged
like a pebble in my throat.
Some messages don’t need replies—
they just need to be held,
quiet as a hand
pressed against a fogged window,
waiting for the shape of you
to step back into view.
I read it again.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: Why Peace Is Not the Absence of Storm
scripts.script:
The kettle hums the same old tune.
It remembers every morning,
even the ones you slept through.
PEACE ISN’T THE ROOM WITHOUT RAIN,
it’s the hand that cups your chin
while thunder argues with the roof.
Your name in my mouth
like a sugar cube dissolving—
slowly,
the sweetness spreads.
I don’t need the sky to clear,
just your palm pressed to my ribs.
send this to them.

You’re folding the same sweater again.
Third time this week.
It still smells like their detergent,
but...
the scent is fading into fabric.
You trace the seams,
remembering how their fingers used to brush yours
when they’d help.
Now the stitches feel loose,
like the way you’ve been unraveling.
Not all at once—
just...
a thread here,
a loop there,
until one day you look down
and it’s just yarn in your hands.
Put it away.

It’s not stillness. It’s holding your breath underwater.
You don’t drown.
The pressure builds,
but your ribs remember how to rise.

A single candle flickers,
and the room doesn’t move with it.
You do.

Your pulse slows,
not like surrender,
like a river learning the shape of its banks.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Kind of Love That Doesn't Shout
scripts.script:
Hafiz whispered
love that doesn’t shout
is the kind
that lingers
in the hush between heartbeats.

It doesn’t need to prove itself
or demand the spotlight—
it simply stays,
soft as dew on morning grass,
unseen but holding everything.

You’ll feel it
when the world forgets to ask.

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
The radio hums
something old
something you loved
before the tears
BEFORE THE SEATBELT
became a noose
pulling tighter
with every breath
You count the cracks
in the dashboard
like rosary beads
until the storm
passes
unseen

How do you explain to someone
that the quietest things leave the loudest marks?
The way a coffee cup still warms your palms,
long after they’ve walked away.
Not the grand gestures,
just the steam curling into morning,
the way it lingers like a half-remembered dream.
Some moments don’t ask for attention,
they settle into your bones
and refuse to be shaken loose.
It’s not the storm that changes you—
it’s the way the rain tastes on your lips.
Let them know.

Hafiz whispered
like a riverbed
that holds no water
but dreams of rain
it waits not for the storm

you too
carry emptiness
that is not lack
but space
for what has not arrived

let the quiet shape you
softly

I stopped translating my sighs into sentences.
The kettle hums,
still waiting for the whistle you never heard.
I trace the rim of my cup
where your name used to steam.
The sugar bowl stays half-empty,
its crystals clinging like unspoken apologies.
Your favorite mug collects dust
on the highest shelf,
just out of reach.
I don’t climb for it anymore.
The tea leaves settle,
finally understanding they’re not needed.
I drink the quiet now,
and it tastes like you.

You don’t own yourself yet.
Not in the way a river owns its bed
or the way salt remembers the ocean.
Some days you wake up
and the mirror shows a stranger
wearing your name like a borrowed coat.
The buttons don’t align,
the sleeves swallow your hands,
but you keep fastening it tighter—
not for warmth,
just to feel something holding you.
Then one morning,
you leave it draped over the chair.
Walk out.
Belong to the air instead.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
There’s a quiet like the first sip of tea,
still too hot to drink.
Your laugh lands
just before the joke,
like a secret you’re already in on.
I notice the way you hold a door,
not for me,
for the next stranger,
and it’s the smallest kindness
that undoes me.
Your hands remember the shape of spoons,
the way mine remember the weight of yours.
Send this to them.

You’re tracing circles on the fogged-up window,
watching your breath make shapes,
but the cold doesn’t bite like it used to.
Some people,
just by existing,
rewrite the temperature of a room.
The way they laugh,
not loud,
just right,
like a key turning in a lock you didn’t know was stuck.
You don’t need to stay,
but you do,
quietly,
like a bookmark holding your place.
They’re the reason you forget to check the time.

The last text still glows on your screen.
not sent,
just saved,
like a secret you keep for them.
Some moments feel endless
until you realize
they were never yours to hold.
The tide takes the footprints,
but leaves the salt behind,
just enough to remember
the way home was wet.
let them go.

The last text still sits unread,
three dots,
then nothing.
Some friendships don’t end with a fight,
just a slow drift
like a boat untied.
You don’t notice the distance
until the shore is a blur,
and the water’s too wide to swim back.
I still check your name in my phone,
not to call,
just to remember the weight of it.
Some people leave without saying goodbye,
and the silence isn’t empty—
it’s full of everything unsaid.

behind my smile
we just
hide our deepest fears
in the folds of our clothes
still searching for a place
to call our own and feel safe
SOMETIMES I FEEL LIKE I'M DROWNING
in the expectations of others
we put on a brave face
still hoping to be seen
and understood by someone

scripts.topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
scripts.script:
The backpack’s still packed,
but the straps don’t fit anymore.
Same weight,
different shoulders.
Some roads split without a sign,
just two pairs of shoes
heading opposite directions.
The jokes still land,
but the laughter falls
into an empty hallway.
I won’t force the zipper closed,
just leave the door unlocked.
They’ll know where to find me.

Broken glass doesn’t stay sharp forever.
It softens,
rolling in the tide,
just grains now,
polished by what crushed it.
Some waves demand surrender,
not to drown,
but to teach the shore how to hold.
The ocean never apologizes,
and neither do you,
carrying its salt like wisdom,
heavy,
but no longer cutting.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
There’s a language in unguarded moments.
Your fingers trace the rim of a cold cup,
slowly,
like it holds the last warmth on earth.
I’ve memorized the tilt of your head
when the world feels too loud,
just...
leaning into nothing,
but holding everything.
Some secrets don’t need words.
They live in the way your shoulders sigh,
unseen,
and I...
I keep them safe.

scripts.topic: When You Love Someone Who Is Leaving
scripts.script:
The suitcase is half-zipped,
unfinished,
like the sentence we started at breakfast.
Some mornings,
the coffee goes cold
before the goodbye is spoken.
I trace your collarbone,
map of places I’ll miss,
each freckle a city
I can’t visit again.
The door clicks shut,
not with anger,
but the soft sigh of a book
closing for the last time.

scripts.topic: When You Delete the Message Before Sending It
scripts.script:
The cursor blinks
backspace
backspace
backspace
and the words dissolve
like sugar in forgotten tea.
YOU HOLD YOUR BREATH
counting the seconds
till the screen goes blank
and no one knows
you almost said it.
The ache lingers
in the space between
typing and undoing.

Home isn’t where you rest.
It’s the way your coffee cup stays warm
long after the last sip.
I trace the rim,
remembering how your hands fit here,
steady and sure.
The clock hums,
but time doesn’t move—
it breathes.
And suddenly,
the air smells like laundry left in the dryer
too long,
soft and familiar.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Someone Chooses You Every Day
scripts.script:
Every morning it’s the same small ritual,
coffee steams,
two spoons,
clinking like quiet promises.
The cup you hand me isn’t just ceramic,
it’s a daily decision,
wrapped in warmth,
stirred with intention.
Some days I forget to notice,
but you never forget to choose,
even when the sugar runs out,
even when the milk sours,
you pour anyway.

It’s not happiness. Not the way they see.
Your smile is a pocket watch,
ticking backward.
I count the seconds between teeth,
trying to read the time.
The gears are quiet,
but I hear the rust.
You wind yourself tighter every morning,
until the spring snaps.
I catch the pieces
before they hit the floor.
Send this to them.

There's a bravery that tastes like rain on dry earth,
unfurling roots before the first bloom.
I press my palm to the window,
watching the sky rewrite its story.
Every beginning is a quiet theft,
stealing back what fear once borrowed.
The door creaks open,
just enough for one foot to step through.
I count the cracks in the sidewalk,
each one a map to somewhere new.
Your name is the first word I practice,
soft against the mirror's edge.
This time,
the pen won't shake.

scripts.topic: When You Are the One Who Always Forgives
scripts.script:
You don’t forgive them. You forgive the space between.
The way old books hold pressed flowers,
but the pages never yellow.
I watch your hands,
unfolding each apology like origami,
giving the paper back its wings.
You are not soft.
You are the hinge that never creaks,
the door left ajar for ghosts who forget to knock.
And I...
I am the dust settling on your shoulders,
learning how to stay.
send this to them.

You don’t fight anymore. Not like that.
The mirror still asks,
but your reflection doesn’t flinch.
A single candle on the sill—
not for prayer,
for remembering how small fires
can rewrite the whole room.
Your spine learns the shape of standing,
not against,
just standing.
The door stays closed.
No slam,
no apology.
Send this to yourself.

You’re tracing their name
on the steam of your coffee cup,
just like you used to
on fogged-up windows.
The shape doesn’t stick,
but your fingers remember the curve.
Some mornings,
you swear you hear their laugh
in the clink of a spoon,
and you turn,
hoping—
but it’s only the neighbor’s dog,
scraping at the door.
You leave the cup half-full.
Let them know.

scripts.topic: When You Cry in the Car Alone
scripts.script:
You're crying in the driver's seat again.
The steering wheel remembers every tear.
Rain taps the roof like Morse code,
but the message is just static.
YOUR FINGERS trace the dashboard cracks,
slowly mapping the fault lines of your ribs.
I wish I could collect each drop
before it stains the upholstery.
THE CAR doesn't ask why,
just holds the shape of your sorrow.
One blinker ticks,
steady as a metronome for breaking.
Send this to them.

You're peeling the label off the bottle,
again.
The glue gives way,
but the shape stays,
like the way you still smile
when their name appears.
Some nights,
the edges curl,
and you don’t press them back.
The kitchen light hums,
just like their voice used to,
but the pitch is wrong now.
You fold the strips,
neatly,
in a drawer no one opens.
Let them go.

The teacup had a chip you always hid.
not from carelessness
but from knowing
you’d be the one to find it again.
Happiness isn’t the full set,
it’s the quiet way you trace the crack
when no one’s watching.
Some truths are older than joy itself—
like how the kettle hums
a tune only your hands remember.
and the steam still rises,
not because it has to,
but because you asked it to.

scripts.topic: The One I Think About First
scripts.script:
The first sip of coffee isn’t yours,
but it warms my throat anyway.
Every morning,
the steam writes your name
in loops I pretend not to trace.
Some rituals
are just muscle memory,
like dialing digits
before remembering the area code changed.
The mug stays,
chipped where you gripped it,
still holding the ghost of your laugh.
I drink to the dregs,
not because I want to,
but because the cup’s already half yours.

Real love isn’t a locked door,
it’s a hallway.
Some nights it stretches,
endless,
just the hum of the fridge,
and the way your breath syncs
to the clock’s second hand.
It doesn’t ask for fireworks,
only the patience to watch
paint dry,
then call it art.

The sugar bowl was half-empty.
You didn’t ask how I take it.
Three spoons in,
you stirred the quiet like a secret.
I watched the grains dissolve—
not sweetness,
just the way you moved.
The kitchen clock ticked,
but time wasn’t counting us.
Your sleeve brushed my wrist,
and the air turned to honey.
I didn’t need to speak.
Send this to them.

I forgot how soft my own voice sounds
when it doesn’t have to fight
to be heard.
The couch cushions remember the shape of me
now,
curving like a question
I finally have time to answer.
YOU USED TO SAY I’D NEVER LAST
five minutes without talking,
but here I am
counting the cracks in the ceiling
like constellations
no one else has named yet.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
You’re pressing your thumb into the bruise
again
like it’s a doorbell you can ring
for answers.
The ache doesn’t lie,
but you keep testing its honesty—
slowly circling the tender spot
until the skin hums with memory.
Some gifts arrive in plain brown paper,
some in the shape of a scar.
You peel back the layers,
and there it is:
the quietest thank-you you ever received.

scripts.topic: When Healing Hurts More Than the Wound
scripts.script:
The stitches pull
too tight
through tender skin
and I flinch
not from the needle
but the memory
of how deep it went.
BANDAGES STICK
to scabs I thought were gone
peeling back layers
of me
I’d already mourned.
It aches
like a limb waking up
alive
for the first time.
visual_keywords: soft lamplight on a hospital gown

scripts.script:
The riverbank remembers
what the water forgot.

you held the shape of an apology
in your cupped hands
until the heat of your palms melted it.

it dripped between your fingers
like candle wax
forming a quiet pool at your feet.

you never looked down.

the puddle dried
but the ground stayed damp
a small patch of earth
still waiting for rain.
visual_keywords: dry cracked earth with damp patch

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
You’re tracing circles on my wrist,
just absentmindedly,
but every loop feels like a secret
I wasn’t supposed to hear.
The way you look at me—
not like I’m glass,
but like I’m the only window
you’ve ever wanted to climb through.
Some nights,
I catch you watching,
and your eyes hold the quiet of a held breath,
like you’re afraid to let it go.
I don’t ask why.
I just memorize the shape of it,
the way your gaze lingers.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
YOU KNOW THE WAY
my hands
shake
when the world feels heavy
like a suitcase I can’t zip
but you
just
lean in
and the weight
softens
into something
I can carry
without looking down.

The last text still says "good morning."
I wake up,
but the pillow smells like detergent now.
Your side of the bed
is just a rectangle of cold sheets.
I trace the outline with my fingers,
pretending it’s a map.
The edges blur,
and suddenly I’m in a city I’ve never visited.
Street signs in a language I almost remember.
I order coffee
black,
just to taste something real.
Send this to them.

YOU TASTED LIKE SUMMER
on the last day of school
when the pool water
was too warm
and the chlorine
stung my throat
not from swimming
but from swallowing
what I couldn’t say.
I kept the towel
we shared
folded in my drawer
until the smell of sunscreen
faded
into nothing.

It’s not hope.
Not even memory.
Just a habit,
like checking the weather
before you step outside—
not because it matters,
but because you’ve always done it.
The screen glows,
soft and familiar,
like a half-remembered song.
You don’t press call.
You don’t delete.
You just… hold the possibility,
like a coin in your pocket,
too heavy to spend,
too precious to lose.

You don’t need me.
Not like that.
The way you reach for my sleeve
when the elevator stalls,
just to check if I’m still there.
Some people are exits,
quietly marked,
but you’re the floor
that doesn’t creak,
the one I step on
without thinking.
Stay for the way you hold a door,
not the way you open it.
Let them know.

The last text still glows on your screen.
Unanswered,
but not deleted.
I trace the letters like old scars,
softly,
knowing the shape of your name
is already changing.
The kettle hums,
but the tea tastes different now.
Not bitter.
Just... new.
I keep the mug anyway.
send this to them.

I stopped counting the freckles on your collarbone.
They map a constellation no telescope can find,
but I trace them in the dark anyway.
Your laugh is the only math I understand—
simple,
addictive,
endless.
The way you stir your coffee clockwise,
three slow circles,
then tap the spoon twice.
I memorized the rhythm,
not the taste.
I never told you the sugar dissolves faster
when you're watching.
send this to them.

You’re not my favorite person.
Not the way honey remembers the comb.
I love you past the last drop,
sticky on fingers,
lingering like a question
no one asks out loud.
The way salt stays after the wave,
not to claim,
but to remind.
You don’t fill me.
You dissolve,
and I keep tasting you
in everything.
send this to them.

Sunday evening. The week hasn’t started yet.
I untangle the knots in my shoulders,
not with force,
but with a sigh.
The body remembers what the mind forgets—
Some cages were never locked.
I step out of the story,
just a character,
no longer the author.
The door stays open,
but I don’t look back.
Freedom isn’t a place.
It’s the quiet click of a latch releasing.

You’re tracing the rim of your coffee cup,
warmth seeps into your fingers,
but the memory still burns.
The first time I knew,
your laugh wasn’t just air—
it was a key,
clicking into a lock I didn’t know was there.
Some doors aren’t meant to open,
just hum,
just vibrate,
just remind you they exist.
I held my breath,
and the whole world tilted,
just for a second.
You were gravity.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
The way your laugh
spills
like loose change from a pocket
clinking down the sidewalk
I pick up each copper moment
and keep it in my palm
YOU NEVER NOTICE
but I do
when your fingers tap the table
in that same old rhythm
like a song you’ve forgotten the words to
and I hum it back
under my breath

scripts.topic: Missing a Person Who Is Still Here
scripts.script:
The extra pillow smells like your shampoo.
I tuck it against my ribs,
pretending the weight is yours.
Your side of the bed stays cool,
even in summer.
I count the freckles on your shoulder
in my mind,
but the numbers keep slipping.
The clock ticks,
and I swear it sounds like your voice
calling me home.
I reach for you,
but the sheets are just cotton.
Send this to them.

Hafiz whispered
this moment is a ripe fig
clasped in your palm
it will split open
if you hold too tight.

I press my thumb into the skin
soft as a temple bell
the juice runs down my wrist
sweet and fleeting
like the name of a childhood friend.

The fig remembers the branch
I remember nothing
but the taste.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in my smile
three years ago
but the weight still spreads like frost
slowly claiming every corner of the room
Some mornings I press my palm to the glass
and watch my breath cloud the world outside
not asking for help
just proving I’m still here
The same hands that lift others from the water
are the ones I use to hold myself underwater
I won’t let the surface break
not today
not for them
just one more ripple quietly fading.

scripts.topic: The Difference Between Living and Existing
scripts.script:
You don’t live here anymore. Just the rooms do.
The kettle still hums at 3 a.m.
but the steam curls into shapes you don’t recognize.
YOUR NAME is written in the condensation,
then erased by the cold.
I trace the letters anyway,
feeling the ghost of your breath on my wrist.
The bed is made. Too neatly.
No one leaves wrinkles like you did,
twisting the sheets into a nest for two.
I sleep on the edge now,
where the mattress dips from memory.
send this to them.

I stopped translating my heart,
just let the words dissolve,
like sugar in rain.
Some tastes don’t need a name,
they linger,
sticky and sweet,
on the tongue of memory.
The hands that once reached,
now rest,
cupped around emptiness.
I won’t rewrite the recipe,
let them wonder
what makes it real.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When Love Feels Like Coming Home
scripts.script:
You leave your shoes by the door
like you belong here
like you never left
and I
I forget how to hold my breath
when your laugh curls around the room
like smoke from a fire we didn’t light
SOMEHOW
it’s warmer here
with you in it
the couch sags in the middle
from all the times we sat too close
and I
I don’t remember why we ever stood apart
visual_keywords: kettle steam on a cold window
fingers brushing dust off a shelf

scripts.script:
WE BREAK
like bread
left too long in the oven
too golden
to be called mistake
just different
now we rise
in ways we never planned
soft inside
hard where it counts
the burn
was the blessing
we carry it
like a second skin.

You leave the last bite
for me
always
like it’s nothing
like the way you hum
off-key in the kitchen
while the coffee percolates
slow and loud
I pretend not to notice
but my chest
softens
just a little.

The porch swing still creaks at midnight.
Not because it’s old,
but because someone remembers
how you liked to sit there,
quietly rocking,
even when the world moved too fast.
Some nights,
the chains hum your favorite song,
and I wonder
if you hear it too,
from wherever you are.
I stay,
not out of habit,
but because the air feels lighter
when I do.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone Who Is Still Alive
scripts.script:
Hafiz whispered
across the table
between two cups of tea
one is still full
one is yours.

you reach for the wrong one
every time.
not because you forget
but because the ache
is the only shape
that fits your hands now.

leave it steeping.

You’re tracing circles on my wrist again.
A compass with no north,
just the quiet pull of your skin.
I didn’t know maps could be this soft,
that your fingers would rewrite every border.
The way you hum—
not a song,
something older,
like the earth remembering rain.
I was a riverbed before you,
dry and waiting,
but you didn’t fill me.
You taught me how to hold the water.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
How do you explain the weight of a river
that no longer flows through you,
but still carves your bones
into a canyon,
Some days you wake up lighter,
just a pebble skipping across the surface,
and other days,
you are the stone
that sinks without sound,
The water remembers your name,
but you learn to float,
not drown,
carry the current,
not the flood.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
You’re tracing circles on my wrist,
like you’re memorizing the map
before the borders change.
I don’t pull away,
just let the ink of your touch
bleed into my pulse.
Some nights,
even the air between us feels solid,
like we’re both holding our breath
to keep the shape of this moment.
You look at me
and I swear,
you’re seeing the parts I’ve never shown.
send this to them.

The last text still glows on your screen.
A single sock hangs in the dryer,
unclaimed.
I trace the shape of your pillow
like a map of places I’ll never go again.
The door clicks,
not a slam,
just the soft sigh of a house
learning to breathe without you.
Your favorite mug sits cold,
full of tea you never drank.
I leave it there,
a quiet monument to the morning we almost had.
The bed feels wider,
but the sheets remember the weight of your body.
I don’t fold them.
Send this to them.

You don’t fight anymore. You just shine.
The way a river doesn’t argue with the stone,
only wears it smooth over centuries.
Your edges soften,
not from surrender,
but from choosing which battles deserve your current.
The bed you make is no longer a trap,
just a place to rest.
And when they ask why you’re different,
you smile,
let the question dissolve like salt in water.
send this to them.

Home isn’t a place you return to.
It’s the way your coffee cup
still fits your palm,
even after years apart.
The kitchen smells like burnt toast
and forgiveness,
but the table only has two chairs now.
You don’t notice the cracks
until you walk barefoot across the floor—
suddenly every splinter
remembers your name.
Some doors don’t close all the way,
and that’s okay.
You learn to live with the draft.

scripts.topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
scripts.script:
YOU CARRY THE ROOF
on your shoulders
while the walls
lean in
whispering your name
like a debt
you never borrowed
but still
pay in sleepless hours
THEY CALL IT STRENGTH
when really
it’s just
another kind of drowning
visual_keywords: a single sock left on the floor

scripts.script:
Misunderstanding is a locked door.
No key,
just the sound of your voice
bouncing off the wood,
unheard.
Some people press their ear to the grain,
but only hear what they already believe.
The hinges don’t creak,
they just stay closed,
while you stand there,
holding the weight of words
that never reached them.
Let them go.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look When You Do Not Know I Am Watching
scripts.script:
You’re not hiding. Just resting.
The way your lashes brush your cheek,
like a moth testing air before flight.
I count the freckles you’ve never seen,
constellations only I know.
Your lips part,
a secret almost spoken.
The room hums,
but you’re somewhere quieter.
A stillness so deep
it feels like falling upward.
I should look away.
I never do.

scripts.topic: The Meaning We Choose to Find
scripts.script:
We are cartographers of the unseen,
marking borders on blank paper,
drawing rivers where none flow,
just to watch them shimmer.
Some maps lead nowhere,
but the ink still sings.
The compass spins,
we choose north anyway,
not because it’s true,
but because we need a direction.
Every line we trace
is a promise we make to the empty sky,
and the sky keeps our secrets.

Fingertips tracing my smile
we just
remember laughter
creases on my face deepen
and somewhere in the chaos
SOMETIMES I FORGET
what's real
still searching
for the truth behind
my eyes that sparkle
FADE INTO THE CROWD
and lose myself

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
There's a quiet like a riverbed,
empty of water
but full of stones you can't lift.
Some currents you can't fight,
just learn to float.
The hands that built the dam
are not yours to unbuild,
but the way you swim
is yours to choose.
Not the depth,
not the pull,
just the breath between waves.
Let them carry what they must.
You carry only yourself.

YOU USED TO MAP EVERY STEP
like a child
counting sidewalk cracks
one two three
until the numbers
lost their shape
and the road
bent without warning
YOU STOPPED
asking for directions
let the detours
hold your hand
now the miles
taste like rain on a stranger’s coat

They’re not drafts. They’re fossils.
Pressing words into the screen,
soft as a thumbprint on clay.
Every backspace is an excavation,
but the bones stay buried.
I trace the shape of your name
like a riverbed I can’t drink from.
The cursor blinks—
a metronome for a song I’ll never sing.
Some love letters aren’t meant to be read.
They’re meant to be.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
The coffee cup stays warm too long.
It used to cool before you spoke,
now it lingers,
holding the shape of your hands.
Some mornings feel like rehearsals,
quiet lines we’ve practiced for years,
but the script keeps changing.
I don’t correct it anymore,
just let the steam rise,
unscripted,
like a breath you almost took.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling
but they still glow in the blue hour
like veins of a map I can’t read
Some nights
I trace them with my finger
and wonder who drew the borders
The voice doesn’t shout anymore
just hums in the background
like a fridge you forget is running
until the power cuts out
I won’t argue with static
I’ll let it play
quiet as a held breath.

scripts.topic: What I'd Tell You If I Could
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling.
Every night they split wider,
like the way my ribs remember your laugh.
I never told you how the sheets still smell
of the laundry detergent you hated,
but I hoard it like stolen perfume.
The clock hums,
its hands moving backward when I close my eyes.
I’d whisper your name into the pillow,
but the feathers might carry it away.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: When You Delete the Message Before Sending It
scripts.script:
The cursor blinks like a guilty heartbeat.
It knows,
but won’t tell.
Three dots appear,
then vanish—
like a breath you almost took.
The words sit heavy in drafts,
not unsaid,
just never sent.
Some confessions don’t need delivery,
they just need to exist.
You delete the message,
but the weight stays.
Like a stone in your pocket.

scripts.topic: When You Realized You Were the Option, Not the Priority
scripts.script:
The voicemail still says your name.
but the voice isn’t yours anymore.
I trace the letters on my screen,
like braille for a language I’ve forgotten.
THE BED IS TOO WIDE,
even when I stretch,
my fingers only brush cold sheets.
You used to leave your socks here,
now the drawer just holds dust.
I keep checking the time,
like you’ll walk in any second.
The clock doesn’t lie.
send this to them.

There's a strength that doesn't roar.
It hums beneath the skin,
like a river carving canyons
without a sound.
Your stillness isn't empty—
it's the space between piano keys,
where the music waits.
I've watched you hold the world
with hands that never tremble,
and wondered if you knew
how beautiful it is
to be unshaken.
send this to them.

You're untangling the knot in your necklace.
The clasp finally gives.
No one told you freedom could be this small.
Your fingers remember the weight,
but your throat doesn't.
A single pearl rolls across the table,
slowly becoming a planet of its own.
You don't chase it.
LET IT GO.
The chain lies coiled like a question
no one needs answered anymore.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Dreams Where They Come Back
scripts.script:
You don’t remember them. You remember the way
the bathtub water held your knees like a promise.
Every night,
the same dream replays:
you find their socks
still balled in the laundry basket,
but the basket is empty.
You press one to your face,
and it smells like nothing.
NOTHING AT ALL.
The faucet drips,
and for a second,
you swear it’s their voice
counting the seconds.
send this to them.

Hafiz knew this truth—
not all who walk beside you
leave footprints on your ribs.
Some arrive
like a tide that smooths the stones
without asking them to bend.
You’ll feel it in the way they hold
not your shape,
but the space you take.
No grand gesture,
just the quiet click of a door
that never locks behind you.
Stay where the air
still tastes like morning.

It’s not pain.
Just a compass you didn’t ask for,
spinning,
pointing nowhere,
but always back to you.
The lessons aren’t gentle—
they arrive like tide,
slowly erasing the footprints
you swore you’d follow forever.
Some days,
you hate the teacher.
Some days,
you thank the waves.
It doesn’t heal.
It just teaches you how to float.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
The coffee cup sits half-empty,
still warm to the touch.
I trace the rim,
counting cracks like continents.
Somewhere between the chipped edge,
and the sugar I forgot to stir,
you exist in negative space.
The chair across from me,
carves absence into wood.
I pour another cup,
pretend it’s for two.
The steam rises,
but never quite reaches you.

Somewhere between the hum of engines,
the city forgot to breathe.
I was a loose bolt,
rattling in the undercarriage,
until your fingers found me.
Not to tighten,
just to listen—
that hollow song I’d been singing.
The road still moves,
but now I roll,
quietly held.
visual_keywords: a single wrench left on asphalt

scripts.script:
The tide doesn’t apologize for leaving,
softly,
just pulls the sand from under your feet
and you’re lighter for a moment.
Some waves,
you swear they knew your name,
but the ocean doesn’t keep receipts.
It gives,
takes,
and moves on
like a letter you wrote but never sent.
The shore remembers the shape of you,
just not how long you stayed.

I stopped counting the cracks in the ceiling
at 3:17 a.m.
when the mattress remembered your shape
still warm
but not yours anymore.
The sheets still smell like rain on pavement
and I press my face deeper
not to forget
just to feel something real.
Some nights the room tilts
and I reach for the wall
like it’s the only thing standing.
I won’t call it home
but it’s the safest place I know.

You're untangling the sweater you both bought in Lisbon.
The yarn snags on your thumbnail,
but the knot won’t loosen.
EACH THREAD remembers the way his fingers used to trace your wrist,
now just a habit you’re unlearning like a language.
You hold the sleeve to your face,
and the wool smells like detergent,
not like him anymore.
SOME THINGS fade without drama,
just the slow dissolve of salt in water.
You fold it neatly,
place it in the giveaway pile.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
YOUR EYES
carry the weight
of every room
we ever shared
I still
find the shape of you
in the way the kettle hums
when it’s almost ready
WE NEVER TALKED
about the way you’d tilt your head
just so
before laughing
like it was a secret
only I could hear
and now
every glance
is a half-remembered melody
I can’t quite place.

There’s a kind of ache
that hums like a fridge in an empty kitchen
You don’t notice it
until the door stays open too long
and the cold spills out
Some hearts love like that
not turning off
just waiting for the light to flicker back on
I trace the shape of you
in the frost on the freezer shelf
not asking for thaw
just memorizing the cracks
let them know.

scripts.topic: When You Make Ordinary Moments Feel Different
scripts.script:
There’s a quiet like the first sip of coffee—
not warmth,
just the promise of waking up.
You leave the mug on the counter,
and suddenly,
the kitchen hums a little louder.
Some mornings,
the clock ticks like it’s keeping a secret,
and you’re the only one listening.
I won’t say it’s magic,
just the way your presence
rewrites the air.
send this to them.

Pain is not the enemy.
It’s the chisel
that carves you deeper,
not to break,
but to hollow you out
for something new.
The cracks don’t vanish,
they become the space
where light used to be,
but now,
just room.
You don’t walk away the same.
You walk away
carrying the shape of what hurt you,
but it’s lighter now.
Send this to them.

YOU KEEP THE CALENDAR,
I keep
forgetting,
YOU REMEMBER
birthdays,
I remember
only the way you sigh
when I miss them.
Somewhere between your reminders
and my empty pockets,
we stopped being two,
started being
one person
carrying the weight,
and the other,
just carrying.

scripts.topic: How to Carry Loss Without Being Crushed
scripts.script:
I carry it like a river stone
polished by time
hard and smooth
but still heavy in my pocket.
Every step makes a tiny sound
like a secret knocking
against my thigh.
I won’t let it drown me
just let it roll
with the rest of my days
until it feels like home.

Hafiz whispered
beneath the laughter
there’s a room
no one enters.

not even you.

you leave the door ajar
let the drafts in
shiver in borrowed warmth
pretend the chill doesn’t crawl up your spine.

sometimes the smile
is just a key
you forgot to turn.

You’re folding the letter again.
The creases know your fingers by heart,
but the paper doesn’t remember the words.
Some truths only fit
when they’re crumpled,
just a shape in your palm,
not a weight on your chest.
The envelope is already sealed,
and the stamp is just a formality—
you’ve been mailing this for years.
Let the mailbox swallow it whole.
It was never yours to keep.

scripts.topic: When You Miss Someone Who Is Still Alive
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that only your voice can feed.
The fridge hums,
empty shelves,
but the ache isn't for food.
I trace the shape of your name
on fogged-up windows,
and the glass stays cold.
Some distances aren't measured in miles.
They stretch between heartbeats,
between the words we don't say.
I press my palm to the phone screen,
like it could warm your absence.
send this to them.

Hafiz whispered
between the cracks of your smile
the body keeps score
not in ink but in sighs
in the weight of a held breath.
You think the world can't see
but the river knows
the way your hands tremble
when you stir your tea.
It's not about fixing
your edges are already soft enough
just let the pretending
rest like a bird
on the railing at dusk.

scripts.topic: The Weight of Being the Strong One
scripts.script:
You’re still holding the ladder.
Not because they asked,
but because you know
if you let go,
they might fall.
The rungs dig into your palms,
steady,
unshaking,
while your arms start to tremble.
Some days,
you forget you’re the one keeping it upright.
And then,
the weight presses down,
not on them—
on you.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What You Cannot Change and What You Can
scripts.script:
You're tracing circles on the steamy bathroom mirror.
The fog remembers your touch,
but the glass stays cold.
SOME THINGS STAY STUBBORNLY THE SAME:
The way your coffee cup fits in my palm,
The exact shade of blue in your winter coat.
I can't change how the rain sounds on your window,
Or how your laugh lingers in empty hallways.
BUT I CAN:
Leave the porch light on when you're late,
Learn the name of every freckle on your shoulder,
Whisper your favorite song when you forget the words.
The mirror will clear.
Send this to them.

The porch swing still rocks,
even when you’re not here.
I don’t push it,
just let the motion
carry the weight of what we used to say.
Some nights,
I trace the cracks in the wood,
counting the years like knots in the grain.
You are the quiet between creaks,
the pause before the chain sighs,
where the world slows,
and I remember how to breathe.
Stay in the stillness with me.

scripts.topic: You Make the Hard Days Easier
scripts.script:
Some days feel like carrying a river,
just the weight of the current in my pockets.
But you,
you show up with a straw,
suck the water out drop by drop,
until my coat feels like paper again.
Not lighter,
just easier to fold,
to tuck into the backseat of a moving car,
while the road hums a lullaby
and the headlights stitch the dark into something soft.

scripts.topic: The Small Things I Notice About You
scripts.script:
You’re stirring your coffee
just to watch the sugar dissolve.
The way your fingers tap the mug
like a secret code only you know.
I notice how you laugh at nothing,
soft and sudden,
as if the joke was whispered in your ear.
Some mornings you hum,
low and off-key,
but the melody fits perfectly.
The crease between your brows
when you’re concentrating on something small—
like folding a napkin just right.
I could list a hundred tiny things,
but it’s the quietest ones that undo me.

You’re tracing circles on my wrist.
A compass you didn’t know you held.
Every loop a promise,
soft as the hum of a fridge at night.
I could map the world by these spirals,
but why would I leave when the center’s here.
Your thumb pauses,
like a needle lifting from vinyl.
The room holds its breath.
Some people are gravity.
Stay.

scripts.topic: Why the Wound Is the Gift
scripts.script:
The potter knows—
the crack is where
the glaze pools deepest,
where the fire lingers
to make the bowl whole.

I trace the thin scar
of that old burn,
remember how the clay
held its shape
through the heat.

It still holds water.

You trace the rim of your coffee cup.
A porcelain moon,
holding the steam like a secret.
Your lips curve,
soft as the first snowfall on pavement.
No one notices the cracks beneath,
the way the heat still burns your tongue.
I see the way your fingers tremble,
just slightly,
when you think no one’s watching.
That smile is a locked door,
and I’ve spent years
trying to pick the lock.
Send this to them.

I stopped counting the ways you fit,
just how the tide remembers the shore,
even when the moon turns away.
Some nights,
I trace the cracks in my ceiling,
and every line leads back to your name.
Not as a map,
but as the shape my ribs make,
holding something too big to name.
You’re not my favorite person,
you’re the quiet weight,
the one my bones still lean toward,
even when I forget to breathe.

There's a strength that hums
beneath the skin of stillness,
like a riverbed holding the weight of sky.
Your breath,
a quiet tide,
pulls me into the fold of your quiet.
I trace the edges of your silence,
learning the shape of what you don’t say.
The room contracts,
but the space between us
feels vast as a held note.
I could stay here,
listening to the quiet strength you carry.
send this to them.

tears fall
we just
without a reason
it's as if something
is unfolding inside
SOMETIMES IT'S A MEMORY
that we can't quite
place still
it lingers
AND SOMETIMES IT'S NOTHING
at all but the ache

It’s not the quiet that scares you.
It’s the way the room remembers your shape.
The couch still holds the dip of your shoulder,
and the kettle hums the same old tune,
but the steam curls like a question mark.
You taught yourself to listen here,
to the way absence fills a space
like breath held too long.
The walls don’t whisper,
they just lean in,
waiting for you to answer.
Send this to them.

How do you describe a place
that doesn’t exist on maps
but lives in your pulse?
The door never locked,
just creaked like a secret
waiting to be told.
Some rooms hold your breath,
others let it out slow,
like a tide learning the shore.
I won’t call it walls
or a roof,
just the way the air
remembers your name.
let them know.

YOU LET THE ROPE GO
slack
and the kite
climbed
past the fence you built
past the rules you wrote
past the ceiling you thought was sky
it didn’t ask
it just
tugged
once
and you
followed

scripts.topic: When Love Feels Like Coming Home
scripts.script:
I stopped counting the steps to your door.
The hallway tilts when you hum,
and my ribs remember the shape of your laugh.
Not a key,
but the way your fingers brush my wrist—
like unlocking something rusted shut.
I never knew home had a temperature,
until your skin met mine.
The kettle whistles,
but it’s just your breath,
steady against my collarbone.
Send this to them.

I stopped counting the cracks in the sidewalk.
Every step a small surrender,
and the concrete still holds me.
I trace the same path,
but the ground remembers my weight differently now.
The dandelions push through,
unafraid of the gaps.
I press my palm to the fence,
its rusted edges warm from the afternoon.
Some journeys don’t need destinations.
Just the quiet hum of being where you are.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: You Didn't Fix Me, You Stayed
scripts.script:
4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
just once,
like a question
no one answers.

You didn’t mend the crack in my cup,
just poured yourself in,
steady,
warm enough to keep it whole.

Some nights I still check for leaks,
but your hands are there,
not to fix,
just to hold.

I didn’t need glue.
I needed you.

scripts.topic: Being Strong for Everyone While Breaking Inside
scripts.script:
You’re holding the kettle again.
The steam curls,
but your hands don’t shake.
Somewhere,
a crack spreads in the porcelain,
invisible.
You pour the tea,
steady voice,
just another morning ritual.
The cup fills,
and so do your eyes,
not from the heat.
Let them drink first.

YOU TOUCHED my wrist
and
my pulse
forgot how to count,
stumbled over every second
after that.
I didn’t know skin could remember
a shape
that wasn’t there yet,
that my ribs could ache
from something
not even real.
Now every clock
still ticks
too slow.

scripts.topic: The Beauty of an Unfinished Life
scripts.script:
There's a hunger that tastes like salt,
not on the tongue,
but in the spaces between your ribs.
The body remembers what the mind won't name—
that ache,
soft as the first page of a book
never finished.
I trace your edges
like a cartographer who knows
the map is already obsolete.
Every line I draw
is a prayer to what stays,
what drifts,
what remains unfinished.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: What I'd Tell You If I Could
scripts.script:
You don’t need me. That’s why I’d whisper.
The way your collarbone holds moonlight,
like a saucer of spilled tea—
I’d memorize the cracks.
Every time you laugh,
your ribs write tiny letters
I’ll never read aloud.
But the ink is still wet.
And I’m the only one who knows
where the paper hides.
Send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
How do you name the space between,
where your eyes meet mine
and suddenly the room tilts,
just slightly,
not enough to fall,
but enough to feel the pull.
Some glances are magnets,
others are mirrors,
and yours—
yours are the hands that rearrange the air,
quietly,
until I’m breathing different.
send this to them.

You’re folding the same shirt again.
The crease won’t hold.
just smoothing the same stubborn wrinkle,
and suddenly,
you realize—
No one’s asking for an ironed explanation.
Some arguments
were never meant to be won,
just released like a breath you’ve been holding
too long in your chest.
The room doesn’t need your reasons.
Let the collar stay soft.

There's a quiet like the first page
before the story starts.
A hesitation in the spine of the book,
the ink still wet on your fingers.
You remember how to hold a pen,
but the words won't come.
The courage isn't in finishing,
just turning to the next blank sheet.
And suddenly,
you write your name again.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: The Voice in Your Head That Says You Are Not Enough
scripts.script:
You’re tracing the cracks in your ceiling.
Tiny fault lines,
like the ones you swear you see in your ribs.
The voice hums,
a tuning fork struck against doubt.
It doesn’t yell,
just vibrates,
steady as the hum of the fridge at 3am.
You press your palm to your chest,
feel the buzz,
not yours,
but something older,
borrowed,
left in the pockets of every coat you’ve ever worn.
The voice isn’t yours to keep.
Send this to them.

You don’t disappear.
Just dissolve a little,
like salt in warm water.
The taste stays,
but the shape is gone.
Some loves are oceans—
you swim,
but never drink the whole sea.
I kept my name
on the shore,
where the waves can’t erase it.
Let them carry the salt,
not the story.

scripts.topic: Being Tired in Ways Sleep Cannot Fix
scripts.script:
You're stirring honey into tea again.
The spoon scrapes the porcelain like tired bones.
No one told you exhaustion could taste this sweet,
but the sugar dissolves,
and your wrists still ache.
THE KETTLE WHISPERS something you can't quite hear,
its steam curling around your fingers like old promises.
You count the cracks in the ceiling,
each one a river your thoughts refuse to cross.
The bed is too wide,
the pillows too soft,
and sleep is just another country you can't afford to visit.
Send this to them.

You don’t remember the day.
Just the way the coffee cup warmed your palms,
steady and small,
like a promise you almost believed.
The clock didn’t stop,
but for three sips,
you weren’t waiting anymore.
Some moments don’t change the world.
They change you
without asking,
soft as a cat’s paw on your knee.
Let them stay.
visual_keywords: a cat curled beside a chair

scripts.script:
They hear the clockwork,
not the gears inside,
spinning out of sync.
Every tick,
a misfire,
a spark they call mistake.
Somewhere a river,
flows backward,
carving canyons no one maps.
The hands that built the bridge,
still drown in the current,
quietly counting cracks.

Familiar walls                             
we just 
find our way back                          
through the smell of freshly baked cookies    
and the sound of laughter that still resonates  
SUNDAY MORNINGS                                    
still feel like a warm hug                      
comforting and reassuring                       
COZY CORNERS                                      
where memories are woven into the fabric        
softly fading like the edges of a favorite book 
and the feeling of being rooted stays with us     
quietly aching

I stopped folding myself into pockets.
The way origami remembers every crease,
I trace the edges of my own ribs now.
Not to hide,
but to know where I end.
The kitchen table holds my coffee cup,
steady as the decision to stay.
No one knocks,
and the quiet hums approval.
I count the freckles on my wrist,
like constellations I finally get to name.
This body,
this breath,
this slow belonging.
send this to them.

4:17 a.m. The kettle hums,
just one note,
repeating,
like the ache behind your ribs.
The mug you hold
is the same one you shared,
but the tea tastes different now.
Some nights,
you trace the cracks in the ceiling,
counting,
not stars,
but the hours you’ve pretended.
The steam rises,
slowly,
like the weight you won’t name.
You pour,
and the cup never fills.

The ocean doesn’t ask the shore to steady it.
It arrives,
just breathing,
and the sand remembers how to hold.
Some waves crash,
some lap,
but yours,
yours are the ones I count.
Not to measure,
just to know I’m still here,
still soft,
still yours.
let them know.

scripts.topic: Missing Someone Who Is Right There
scripts.script:
Your laughter still hums
in the kitchen tiles
I press my palm to the counter
warm where you leaned
LAST NIGHT
we built a pillow fort
and forgot to sleep
now the sheets smell like you
I tuck them tighter
pretending
it’s your arms
not the linen
that holds me

I stopped leaving fingerprints on your door,
just traces of salt where my cheek rested.
The air still hums,
but only when you’re near.
I memorized the shape of your absence,
like a keyhole I press my eye to,
not to enter,
just to feel the cold metal.
Some nights I swear I hear your breath,
steady as a metronome counting,
not the music,
but the space between notes.
I won’t ask for a melody,
just let me hold the silence after.
send this to them.

I FORGOT HOW TO HOLD IT
like a kite string
too tight
and the sky
pulled away anyway
so I opened my palm
watched the frayed end
drift
upward
without me
still tangled
in the clouds.

scripts.topic: When You Outgrow Your Best Friend
scripts.script:
We were two rivers
flowing side by side,
carving the same canyon,
until the land shifted.
One path turned,
steep and unfamiliar,
while the other stayed,
flat and known.
I didn’t choose the climb,
just the need to keep moving.
Some currents,
once entwined,
now pull in opposite directions.
The water still remembers,
but the banks have changed shape.

scripts.topic: The Way You Look at Me
scripts.script:
Your eyes hold a room
I’ve never entered
just
the way you tilt your head
makes my ribs hum
like a tuning fork
forgotten in a drawer
I don’t know the song
but my bones remember
the vibration
and now every time you blink
I’m tuning
to something
I can’t name.
visual_keywords: a sweater left on a chair

scripts.script:
I TORE the map
into confetti
threw it
at the ceiling
watched it stick
like static on old TVs
YOU LEFT a hole
in the shape of a question mark
I filled it
with my own name
written in lipstick
on the bathroom mirror
NOW the reflection
doesn’t flinch
when I say it aloud.

scripts.topic: When Just Being Near You Is Enough
scripts.script:
You’re tracing circles on the table,
not listening
just feeling the heat of my sleeve
against yours.
Some moments
are too fragile for words—
like holding a butterfly
that might not stay,
and choosing not to move.
The air between us
is a slow dance of breath,
quiet as the pause
between thunder and rain.
I don’t need to speak.
You already know.

You’re folding laundry at midnight.
The dryer hums like a lullaby,
but the socks never match.
YOU USED TO PLAY dress-up in these,
now they’re just something to pair.
The basket tips,
spills childhood across the floor.
YOU COUNT the years in stains,
in the way the fabric thins.
Somewhere,
a stuffed rabbit watches,
still waiting for its turn.
send this to them.

scripts.topic: Falling For Someone Who Does Not Know
scripts.script:
You’re tracing circles on your thigh
like you’re drawing a map
to somewhere you’ve never been
but they live there
The ink is your pulse
slowly bleeding through your skin
No one sees the lines
just the way your fingers tremble
when their name comes up
Some moments feel like a held breath
and you’re the only one underwater
let them know.

The river knows,
carving canyons without a sound.
It doesn’t push,
just lets the stone surrender,
smooth and slow.
Some hands,
meant to hold,
clutch only air.
And that’s okay.
The current doesn’t apologize,
just carries what it’s given,
leaves the rest to time.

The river doesn’t ask for a map,
just bends where the earth tells it to.
Some days it rushes,
others, it barely moves,
but it never stops trusting the pull.
I used to fight the current,
clinging to rocks that cut my hands,
until I realized,
the water always finds its way.
Now I float,
letting the journey hold me,
knowing the shore will come.

You stayed too long. That’s the problem.
The teacup’s still warm in your hands
but the leaves have settled,
quiet as regret.
I WATCH the steam curl into nothing
and wonder if you taste the same.
Some flavors linger past their welcome,
like your laugh in the hallway,
soft as the last page of a book
I keep meaning to close.
The door’s still open.
Send this to them.

I never told you how the teacup trembled.
Your fingers brushed mine,
and the steam curled into questions.
The porcelain remembered your touch longer than I did.
I counted the cracks,
each one a secret I couldn’t keep.
The sugar never dissolved the same after that.
I stir it now,
and the spoon hums your name.
The kettle’s whistle is just static.
I drink the memory,
bitter and sweet,
send this to them.
